indicators.desc_en: Measures the change in prices paid by urban consumers for a basket of goods and services. The year-over-year change is the most-watched inflation metric in the US. Above 2% signals elevated inflation; the Fed targets 2% over time.
indicators.desc_zh: 衡量城市消費者購買商品和服務的價格變化。年增率是美國最受關注的通膨指標。超過 2% 代表通膨偏高，聯準會的長期通膨目標為 2%。

indicators.name_zh: 核心 CPI（不含食品與能源）
indicators.desc_en: CPI excluding volatile food and energy prices. Core CPI is the Fed's preferred short-term inflation gauge because it filters out supply-shock noise. Persistently high core CPI is a strong signal for rate hikes.
indicators.desc_zh: 剔除波動較大的食品和能源的 CPI。核心 CPI 是聯準會偏好的短期通膨指標，因為它過濾了供給衝擊的噪音。核心 CPI 持續偏高是升息的強烈訊號。

drivers_zh: 生產者物價指數上升1.65反映成本壓力

indicators.name_zh: 核心 PCE 物價指數
indicators.desc_en: The Fed's officially preferred inflation measure. PCE covers a broader range of spending than CPI and adjusts for substitution effects. The FOMC explicitly targets 2% Core PCE as its long-run goal.
indicators.desc_zh: 聯準會官方偏好的通膨衡量指標。PCE 涵蓋比 CPI 更廣泛的消費支出，且會調整替代效應。聯邦公開市場委員會明確以 2% 核心 PCE 為長期目標。

indicators.desc_en: Measures average change in prices received by domestic producers. PPI is a leading indicator for CPI—rising producer costs often get passed on to consumers. A surge in PPI often precedes consumer inflation by 1-3 months.
indicators.desc_zh: 衡量國內生產者收到的價格平均變化。PPI 是 CPI 的領先指標，生產成本上升往往會轉嫁給消費者，PPI 飆升通常比消費通膨早 1-3 個月出現。

indicators.name_zh: 密大消費者通膨預期（一年期）
indicators.desc_en: University of Michigan survey measuring consumer expectations for inflation over the next year. Anchored inflation expectations are critical for central bank credibility. A jump above 4% raises concern about a wage-price spiral.
indicators.desc_zh: 密西根大學調查消費者對未來一年通膨的預期。通膨預期是否穩定對央行公信力至關重要，若飆破 4% 將引發工資-物價螺旋上升的疑慮。

indicators.desc_en: Number of jobs added to the economy (ex-farm & household). The most market-moving monthly release. Above +150K is generally healthy; below +100K signals slowdown. The Fed watches NFP closely alongside inflation.
indicators.desc_zh: 美國經濟新增的非農就業人數，是每月最撼動市場的數據。超過 +15 萬通常為健康；低於 +10 萬則暗示放緩，聯準會高度關注此數據。

drivers_zh: 初領失業金增3千顯疲弱

indicators.desc_en: Percentage of the labor force that is unemployed and actively seeking work. At 3.5-4%, the labor market is considered near-full employment. The Fed's dual mandate includes maximizing employment.
indicators.desc_zh: 正在積極求職的勞動力佔比。3.5-4% 附近被認為接近充分就業。聯準會的雙重任務包含最大化就業。

indicators.name_zh: 初次申請失業救濟金人數
indicators.desc_en: Weekly count of newly unemployed individuals filing for benefits. The most timely labor market indicator. A 4-week moving average above 300K often signals labor market deterioration. Markets are sensitive to sharp week-to-week moves.
indicators.desc_zh: 每週新申請失業救濟金的人數，是最即時的勞動市場指標。四週移動平均超過 30 萬通常代表勞動市場惡化，市場對每週大幅變動極為敏感。

indicators.desc_en: Percentage of working-age population that is employed or actively job-seeking. Low participation can mask real unemployment. Post-COVID, the US LFPR has recovered but remains below pre-pandemic highs due to aging demographics.
indicators.desc_zh: 工作年齡人口中就業或積極求職的比例。低參與率可能掩蓋真實失業情況。疫情後美國勞動參與率已回升，但因人口老化，仍低於疫前高點。

indicators.desc_en: Broadest measure of labor underutilization, including part-time workers who want full-time jobs and marginally attached workers. U-6 typically runs 3-5 percentage points above U-3 (headline unemployment). A narrowing U3/U6 gap indicates a tightening labor market.
indicators.desc_zh: 最廣義的勞動力未充分利用指標，包含希望全職工作卻只能兼職者及邊緣勞工。U-6 通常比 U-3（標準失業率）高 3-5 個百分點，兩者差距收窄代表勞動市場緊俏。

indicators.desc_en: Inflation-adjusted total output of the US economy. Two consecutive quarters of negative real GDP growth constitutes a technical recession. Healthy growth is generally 2-3% annualized. This is the definitive measure of economic health.
indicators.desc_zh: 經通膨調整的美國經濟總產出。連續兩季負成長即為技術性衰退。健康的成長率約為年化 2-3%，是最終極的經濟健康指標。

forecast.metric_zh: 年化實質 GDP 成長
drivers: Atlanta Fed GDPNow is the official running nowcast of current-quarter GDP growth.
drivers_zh: GDPNow 即時估計（亞特蘭大聯儲） 官方即時估計:+1.19
亞特蘭大聯儲 GDPNow 是當季 GDP 成長的官方即時估計。

indicators.desc_en: Monthly measure of consumer spending at retail stores, excluding volatile auto sales. Consumer spending drives ~70% of US GDP, so strong retail sales signal economic resilience. A sharp miss can trigger equity market selloffs.
indicators.desc_zh: 每月零售商場消費支出，剔除波動較大的汽車銷售。消費支出佔美國 GDP 約 70%，強勁的零售銷售代表經濟韌性，大幅低於預期可能引發股市賣壓。

drivers_zh: 消費者信心大幅下跌5點

indicators.desc_en: Measures output of US factories, mines, and utilities. Industrial production accounts for about 15% of GDP but is more volatile and leads economic turning points. A sustained decline often precedes recession.
indicators.desc_zh: 衡量美國工廠、礦業和公用事業的產出。工業生產佔 GDP 約 15%，波動較大但能領先經濟轉折點，持續下滑往往是衰退先兆。

indicators.desc_en: Total spending by US households on goods and services. PCE is broader than retail sales and is the primary component of GDP. A deceleration in PCE growth warns of economic softening even when headline unemployment stays low.
indicators.desc_zh: 美國家庭在商品和服務上的總支出，比零售銷售更廣泛，是 GDP 的主要組成部分。PCE 成長放緩是經濟趨軟的警示，即使失業率仍低。

indicators.desc_en: The interest rate at which banks lend reserve balances to each other overnight—the primary policy tool of the Federal Reserve. Rate hikes cool inflation but risk slowing growth; cuts stimulate the economy. Every basis point move ripples across all asset classes.
indicators.desc_zh: 銀行間隔夜拆借準備金的利率，是聯準會的主要政策工具。升息可抑制通膨但有壓縮成長的風險；降息可刺激經濟。每一個基點的變動都會波及所有資產類別。

indicators.desc_en: Number of new residential construction projects begun per month. Housing is a leading indicator—builders break ground based on demand expectations 6-18 months out. A drop in starts signals a cooling market well before prices adjust.
indicators.desc_zh: 每月開始動工的新住宅建設數量。房屋建設是領先指標，建商依據 6-18 個月後的需求預期動工。開工數下降是房市降溫的早期訊號，早於房價反應。

indicators.desc_en: Number of new construction permits issued monthly. Permits are issued before construction starts, making them an even more leading indicator than housing starts—often 1-2 months ahead. A permit trend closely tracks future housing supply.
indicators.desc_zh: 每月核發的新建築許可數量。許可在施工前核發，比新屋開工數更具領先性，通常早 1-2 個月反映趨勢，許可趨勢緊密追蹤未來住宅供給。

indicators.desc_en: Monthly count of completed sales of previously constructed homes (seasonally adjusted annual rate). Existing home sales account for ~85% of total home sales and reflect real-time demand. A decline signals affordability stress, usually from high mortgage rates or prices.
indicators.desc_zh: 每月已建成住宅的成交量（季調年化）。佔全美房屋銷售約 85%，反映即時需求。銷量下滑代表購房負擔壓力增大，通常源於高房貸利率或高房價。

indicators.desc_en: Average interest rate for a 30-year fixed mortgage in the US. Mortgage rates typically move with the 10-year Treasury yield plus a spread. Above 7% significantly pressures housing affordability and can freeze transaction volume.
indicators.desc_zh: 美國 30 年期固定房貸平均利率。房貸利率通常隨 10 年期美債殖利率加上利差波動，超過 7% 會顯著壓縮購房可負擔性並可能凍結成交量。

indicators.desc_en: The gold standard for tracking US home price changes across 20 major metropolitan areas using a repeat-sales methodology. Published with a 2-month lag. YoY changes above 10% signal speculative excess; consecutive monthly declines signal a correction.
indicators.desc_zh: 追蹤 20 個主要大都會區房價變化的黃金標準指標，採用重複銷售方法論。發布有約 2 個月滯後。年增率超過 10% 代表投機過熱，連續月跌代表房市修正。

indicators.desc_en: Total US manufacturing sector employment (BLS payrolls). A leading indicator for the goods-producing economy. Sustained YoY declines have historically preceded broader recessions.
indicators.desc_zh: 美國製造業總就業人數（勞工部薪資調查）。商品生產經濟的領先指標，年增率持續為負通常領先整體衰退。

indicators.desc_en: Monthly value of new orders placed for manufactured goods expected to last 3+ years (aircraft, machinery, vehicles). Highly volatile due to aircraft orders. The ex-defense, ex-aircraft 'core capital goods' measure is preferred for clean business investment trends.
indicators.desc_zh: 每月製造業新接的耐久財訂單（使用 3 年以上如飛機、機械、車輛）。因飛機訂單而波動極大。剔除國防和飛機的「核心資本財」指標更能反映乾淨的商業投資趨勢。

indicators.desc_en: Index measuring the real output of the manufacturing sector (subset of total industrial production). Captures factory floor activity in real time. A sustained decline in manufacturing production often precedes broader economic slowdowns by 2-3 quarters.
indicators.desc_zh: 衡量製造業實際產出的指數（工業生產總指數的子集）。即時捕捉工廠生產動態。製造業生產持續下滑，往往比整體經濟放緩早 2-3 季出現。

indicators.desc_en: Monthly University of Michigan survey of consumer confidence about current and future economic conditions. Acts as both a coincident and leading indicator—a sharp drop in sentiment can become self-fulfilling as consumers pull back on spending.
indicators.desc_zh: 密西根大學每月調查消費者對當前和未來經濟狀況的信心，兼具同步和領先指標功能。信心大幅下滑可能自我實現，因為消費者會縮減支出。

indicators.desc_en: The spread between 10-year and 2-year Treasury yields. An inverted curve (negative spread) has preceded every US recession since 1955 by 6-18 months. Re-steepening from inversion is often the most reliable recession imminence signal.
indicators.desc_zh: 10年期與2年期美債殖利率差。曲線倒掛（負值）自1955年以來每次都領先美國經濟衰退6-18個月。從倒掛回正陡峭化，是衰退迫近的最可靠訊號之一。

indicators.desc_en: The 10-year minus 3-month Treasury spread—the NY Fed's preferred recession indicator. Inversion has accurately predicted 8 of the last 8 US recessions. Watched more closely by economists than 10Y-2Y.
indicators.desc_zh: 10年期減3個月期美債利差，為紐約聯儲偏好的衰退指標。倒掛準確預測過去8次美國衰退中的全部8次，經濟學家比10年-2年利差更看重此指標。

indicators.desc_en: Yield on the benchmark 10-year US Treasury note. Drives mortgage rates, corporate borrowing costs, and is the global risk-free benchmark. Rising 10Y yields pressure equity valuations, especially high-duration tech stocks.
indicators.desc_zh: 10年期美國國債的基準殖利率，是房貸、企業借貸成本的指標，也是全球無風險利率基準。10年殖利率上升會壓縮股市估值，尤其衝擊長存續期的科技股。

indicators.desc_en: Yield on 2-year Treasury notes, closely tracking Fed policy expectations. The 2-year reflects what markets expect the Fed Funds rate to average over the next two years. Sharp moves often telegraph FOMC pivots before they happen.
indicators.desc_zh: 2年期美債殖利率，緊密追蹤聯準會政策預期。反映市場對未來兩年聯邦基金利率平均水準的預期，大幅波動常領先 FOMC 政策轉向。

indicators.desc_en: CBOE Volatility Index, measuring implied volatility on S&P 500 options. Below 15 = complacency; 20-30 = caution; above 40 = panic. VIX spikes correlate with sharp equity selloffs and flight-to-safety flows.
indicators.desc_zh: 芝加哥選擇權交易所波動率指數，衡量標普500選擇權的隱含波動率。低於15代表市場自滿，20-30為警戒，高於40為恐慌。VIX飆升常伴隨股市大跌與避險資金流入。

indicators.desc_en: ICE BofA US High Yield Index Option-Adjusted Spread—the premium investors demand to hold junk bonds vs. Treasuries. Below 400bps = risk-on; above 600bps = stress; above 1000bps = recession-grade panic. Leading indicator of credit cycle turns.
indicators.desc_zh: ICE BofA 美國高收益債券指數選擇權調整利差，投資人持有垃圾債相對美債所要求的風險溢酬。低於 400bps 為風險偏好，高於 600bps 為壓力，超過 1000bps 為衰退級恐慌。是信用循環轉折的領先指標。

indicators.desc_en: Fed's broad trade-weighted dollar index against major trading partners' currencies. A strong dollar pressures multinational earnings, commodity prices (negatively), and emerging market debt. A weakening dollar typically boosts US equities and EM assets.
indicators.desc_zh: 聯準會編製的貿易加權廣義美元指數，對美國主要貿易夥伴貨幣加權。強勢美元壓縮跨國企業獲利、商品價格與新興市場債務；弱勢美元通常利多美股與新興市場資產。

indicators.desc_en: West Texas Intermediate crude oil spot price per barrel. Oil prices feed into inflation, transportation costs, and consumer discretionary spending. Sustained moves above $90 typically pressure CPI; below $60 raises concerns about global demand.
indicators.desc_zh: 西德州中級原油現貨價格（每桶美元）。油價影響通膨、運輸成本與消費者可支配支出。持續高於 90 美元常推高 CPI；低於 60 美元則引發全球需求疑慮。

indicators.desc_en: Broad measure of US money supply including currency, deposits, and money market funds. M2 YoY growth correlates with inflation 12-18 months ahead. Post-2022 contraction was the first since the Great Depression, signaling tight liquidity.
indicators.desc_zh: 美國廣義貨幣供給，含現金、存款與貨幣市場基金。M2 年增率領先通膨 12-18 個月。2022 年後的萎縮是自大蕭條以來首見，反映流動性緊縮。

indicators.desc_en: Job Openings and Labor Turnover Survey from BLS—total unfilled job postings. The job openings to unemployed ratio (currently watched closely by Powell) signals labor market tightness. A drop below 1.0 typically precedes wage disinflation.
indicators.desc_zh: 勞工部 JOLTS 調查的未填補職位總數。職位空缺對失業人數比率（鮑爾密切關注的指標）反映勞動市場緊俏程度，低於 1.0 通常領先工資通膨降溫。

drivers_zh: 四至五月上升動能延續

indicators.desc_en: Average hourly earnings of all private-sector employees. YoY growth above 4% is consistent with sticky 2%+ services inflation. Fed watches wage growth as the lynchpin of services inflation dynamics.
indicators.desc_zh: 民間部門全體勞工平均時薪。年增率高於 4% 與黏性 2%+ 服務業通膨一致。聯準會將工資成長視為服務業通膨動態的關鍵指標。

indicators.desc_en: Percentage of industrial capacity actually in use. Above 80% indicates tight capacity (inflationary pressure); below 75% signals slack (deflationary). Recession averages historically run 73-75%.
indicators.desc_zh: 工業產能實際使用比例。高於 80% 代表產能吃緊（通膨壓力），低於 75% 代表閒置（通縮壓力）。歷史衰退期平均落在 73-75%。

indicators.name_zh: 芝加哥聯儲全國活動指數 CFNAI
indicators.desc_en: A weighted average of 85 monthly indicators of US national economic activity. Zero equals trend growth; the 3-month moving average below −0.7 has historically signaled the onset of recession, while readings above +0.7 (after an expansion) point to rising inflation pressure.
indicators.desc_zh: 由 85 項全美經濟活動月指標加權而成。0 代表趨勢成長；三個月移動平均低於 −0.7 歷史上預示衰退開始，高於 +0.7（在擴張期後）則暗示通膨壓力升高。

indicators.desc_en: Difference between US exports and imports of goods/services. Persistent deficits (currently ~$70B/month) pressure the dollar long-term. Sudden widening can signal domestic demand strength or import surges (often China-related).
indicators.desc_zh: 美國商品與服務出口減進口的差額。持續赤字（目前約每月 700 億美元）長期施壓美元。突然擴大可能反映內需強勁或進口激增（常與中國貿易相關）。

indicators.desc_en: BLS import price index covering all imported goods. A leading indicator for goods CPI—rising import prices typically pass through to consumer inflation within 1-3 quarters. Closely watched during trade-tension episodes.
indicators.desc_zh: 勞工部編製的全進口商品價格指數，是商品 CPI 的領先指標。進口物價上升通常 1-3 季後轉嫁至消費通膨，在貿易摩擦時期備受關注。

indicators.desc_en: Headline Personal Consumption Expenditures price index — the broad companion to Core PCE that includes food and energy. The Fed targets 2% on PCE; the headline-vs-core gap reveals how much energy and food are distorting the inflation picture.
indicators.desc_zh: 整體個人消費支出物價指數 — 核心 PCE 的廣義版本，含食品與能源。聯準會以 PCE 2% 為目標；整體與核心的差距揭示能源與食品對通膨圖像的扭曲程度。

drivers_zh: 四至五月加速趨勢延續

indicators.desc_en: Producer Price Index for final demand — the modern headline PPI the BLS features, measuring prices producers receive for finished goods and services. A cleaner CPI lead than all-commodities PPI because it tracks the same final-demand basket as consumer inflation.
indicators.desc_zh: 最終需求生產者物價指數 — 勞工部主推的現代 PPI 標題數字，衡量生產者就成品與服務收到的價格。比全商品 PPI 更乾淨的 CPI 領先指標，因為它追蹤與消費通膨相同的最終需求籃子。

indicators.desc_en: Market-implied average inflation over the next 10 years, from the gap between nominal and TIPS Treasury yields. A real-time, daily read on inflation expectations — when breakevens drift above ~2.5% the Fed worries expectations are un-anchoring.
indicators.desc_zh: 由名目公債與抗通膨債（TIPS）殖利率差推算的市場隱含未來十年平均通膨。即時、每日更新的通膨預期讀數 — 當平衡通膨率漂升超過約 2.5%，聯準會會擔心預期脫錨。

indicators.name_zh: 一年期預期通膨（克里夫蘭聯儲）
indicators.desc_en: Cleveland Fed model estimate of expected inflation over the next year, blending market data, surveys, and inflation swaps. A forward-looking complement to realized CPI/PCE — leading the actual prints rather than confirming them.
indicators.desc_zh: 克里夫蘭聯儲模型估計的未來一年預期通膨，融合市場數據、調查與通膨交換。是已實現 CPI/PCE 的前瞻性補充 — 領先實際數據而非事後確認。

indicators.desc_en: Number of people still collecting unemployment benefits after their initial claim (insured unemployment). While initial claims show new layoffs, continued claims show how hard it is to find re-employment — a rising trend signals a softening labor market even before the unemployment rate moves.
indicators.desc_zh: 初次申請後仍持續領取失業救濟金的人數（受保失業）。初領反映新裁員，續領則反映再就業的難易度 — 續領上升趨勢在失業率變動前就預示勞動市場轉弱。

indicators.desc_en: Share of employed workers who voluntarily quit each month. The quits rate is a confidence gauge — people quit when they're sure they can find a better job. A falling quits rate leads wage-growth deceleration and signals a cooling labor market.
indicators.desc_zh: 每月自願離職勞工佔就業人數比例。離職率是信心指標 — 人們有把握找到更好工作時才會離職。離職率下滑領先工資成長放緩，預示勞動市場降溫。

indicators.desc_en: Share of total employment represented by new hires each month. Together with the quits and openings rates it completes the JOLTS labor-flow picture; a declining hires rate is an early sign that firms are pulling back on expansion.
indicators.desc_zh: 每月新進員工佔總就業人數比例。與離職率、職缺率共同構成 JOLTS 勞動流動全貌；招聘率下滑是企業收手擴張的早期跡象。

indicators.desc_en: Total income received by US households from all sources (wages, investments, transfers). Personal income drives future consumption — sustained growth supports spending, while stagnation warns that consumers will eventually retrench.
indicators.desc_zh: 美國家庭來自各來源（薪資、投資、移轉性收入）的所得總額。個人所得驅動未來消費 — 持續成長支撐支出，停滯則警示消費者終將縮手。

indicators.name_zh: 實質個人所得（不含移轉）
indicators.desc_en: Inflation-adjusted personal income excluding government transfer payments — one of the four coincident indicators the NBER uses to date recessions. Stripping out transfers and inflation isolates the underlying earning power of the private economy.
indicators.desc_zh: 剔除政府移轉性支付、經通膨調整的個人所得 — NBER 用以判定衰退起訖的四大同步指標之一。剔除移轉與通膨後，能分離出民間經濟的根本賺取能力。

indicators.desc_en: Personal saving as a share of disposable income. A falling saving rate (consumers dipping into savings to keep spending) can flatter near-term GDP but warns of fragile future demand; a rising rate signals caution and a coming spending pullback.
indicators.desc_zh: 個人儲蓄佔可支配所得比例。儲蓄率下降（消費者動用存款維持支出）短期可美化 GDP，卻警示未來需求脆弱；上升則代表謹慎與即將到來的支出收縮。

indicators.desc_en: Output per hour in the nonfarm business sector. Productivity growth is what lets the economy grow without inflation — strong productivity (e.g., from AI adoption) raises non-inflationary speed limits, while weak productivity makes wage gains inflationary.
indicators.desc_zh: 非農企業部門每工時產出。生產力成長讓經濟得以在不引發通膨下成長 — 強勁生產力（例如 AI 導入）拉高非通膨的成長上限，疲弱生產力則使工資增長轉為通膨。

indicators.desc_en: Monthly sales of newly constructed single-family homes (seasonally adjusted annual rate). New home sales are recorded at contract signing, making them more timely than existing-home sales (recorded at closing) and a sharper read on current housing demand.
indicators.desc_zh: 每月新建單戶住宅銷售量（季調年化）。新屋銷售在簽約時計入，比成屋銷售（成交時計入）更即時，是當前房市需求更敏銳的讀數。

indicators.desc_en: How many months it would take to sell the current inventory of new homes at the current sales pace. Around 6 months is balanced; above ~7-8 signals oversupply and price weakness ahead, below ~4 signals a tight, builder-favorable market.
indicators.desc_zh: 以當前銷售速度賣完現有新屋庫存所需的月數。約 6 個月為均衡；高於約 7-8 代表供給過剩、房價將走弱，低於約 4 代表市場吃緊、有利建商。

indicators.desc_en: Inflation-protected 10-year Treasury yield — the 'real' cost of money after expected inflation. Real yields are the truest gauge of monetary tightness; rising real yields pressure gold, long-duration tech equities, and rate-sensitive assets the hardest.
indicators.desc_zh: 抗通膨 10 年期公債殖利率 — 扣除預期通膨後的「實質」資金成本。實質殖利率是貨幣緊縮程度最真實的指標；實質殖利率上升對黃金、長存續期科技股與利率敏感資產衝擊最大。

indicators.name_zh: 聯準會資產負債表（總資產）
indicators.desc_en: Total assets held by the Federal Reserve. Expansion (QE) injects liquidity that lifts risk assets; contraction (QT) drains it. The trajectory of the balance sheet is a core driver of the system-wide liquidity tide that moves equities and crypto.
indicators.desc_zh: 聯準會持有的總資產。擴張（QE）注入流動性、推升風險資產；收縮（QT）則抽走流動性。資產負債表的走向是牽動股市與加密貨幣的全系統流動性潮汐的核心驅力。

indicators.desc_en: ICE BofA US Corporate (investment-grade) Option-Adjusted Spread — the premium over Treasuries for high-quality corporate debt. A calmer, slower-moving credit gauge than high-yield; widening IG spreads signal stress reaching even the strongest borrowers.
indicators.desc_zh: ICE BofA 美國投資級公司債選擇權調整利差 — 高評等公司債相對美債的溢酬。比高收益債更平穩、變動更慢的信用指標；投資級利差擴大代表壓力已蔓延至最強健的借款人。

indicators.desc_en: Yield on 3-month Treasury bills — the short end that tracks current Fed policy almost one-to-one. The anchor of the yield curve and the reference for money-market rates; its level relative to the 10-year defines curve inversion.
indicators.desc_zh: 3 個月期國庫券殖利率 — 幾乎一對一追蹤當前聯準會政策的短端。是殖利率曲線的錨點與貨幣市場利率的參考；相對 10 年期的水準定義了曲線倒掛。

indicators.name_zh: GDPNow 即時估計（亞特蘭大聯儲）
indicators.desc_en: The Atlanta Fed's running nowcast of current-quarter real GDP growth, updated several times a month as each piece of source data arrives. It IS the official answer to 'what will GDP print?' weeks before the BEA's first estimate — the anchor of this dashboard's GDP forecast.
indicators.desc_zh: 亞特蘭大聯儲對當季實質 GDP 成長的即時估計，每月隨各項來源數據陸續發布而更新數次。它就是在 BEA 首次估計前數週對「GDP 會是多少」的官方答案 — 本看板 GDP 預測的錨點。

indicators.desc_en: A weekly composite of ten daily/weekly series (retail, claims, rail, electricity, etc.), scaled to track year-over-year GDP growth. Because it updates every week, the WEI flags turning points in activity well before monthly and quarterly data confirm them.
indicators.desc_zh: 由十項每日/每週數據（零售、申領、鐵路、用電等）組成的每週綜合指標，校準至追蹤 GDP 年增率。因每週更新，WEI 在月度與季度數據確認前就標示出活動轉折點。

indicators.desc_en: Triggers when the 3-month average unemployment rate rises 0.50pp above its trailing 12-month low — a remarkably reliable real-time recession start signal. Reading the gap below 0.50 is reassuring; a climb toward 0.50 is the labor market's clearest early warning.
indicators.desc_zh: 當三個月平均失業率較過去 12 個月低點上升 0.50 個百分點時觸發 — 是極可靠的即時衰退起點訊號。讀數低於 0.50 令人安心；逼近 0.50 則是勞動市場最明確的早期警訊。

indicators.name_zh: 芝加哥聯儲金融狀況指數 NFCI
indicators.desc_en: A weekly index of 105 measures of risk, credit, and leverage. Zero equals average conditions; positive = tighter-than-average (headwind), negative = looser-than-average (tailwind). A broad, forward-looking read on whether financial plumbing is helping or hurting growth.
indicators.desc_zh: 由 105 項風險、信用與槓桿指標組成的每週指數。0 代表平均狀況；正值＝較平均緊（逆風），負值＝較平均鬆（順風）。是金融體系對成長是助力或阻力的廣義前瞻讀數。

commentary.zh: 5月CPI指數達333.979，月增0.5%，年增率約4.2%，明顯高於聯準會2%目標。自2025年5月以來，指數累計上升4.2%，反映通膨壓力持續。特別是3月至5月三個月內，指數躍升3.7個點，加速趨勢令人關注。若年增率維持在4%以上，聯準會可能延後降息計畫，支持美元並可能抑制風險資產。市場應密切關注供給面衝擊與需求動能的互動。
commentary.en: May's CPI reading of 333.979 marks a 0.5% monthly gain, translating to roughly 4.2% year-over-year—substantially above the Fed's 2% target. Since May 2025, the index has climbed 4.2%, with acceleration evident in the past three months (up 3.7 points from March). This persistent above-target inflation likely pressures the Fed to maintain restrictive policy longer, potentially delaying rate cuts. Hawkish expectations could support the dollar while dampening equities and growth-sensitive sectors. Monitoring supply-side factors remains critical.

ai.zh: CPI達333.979且持續上升趨勢（月增0.5%）顯示通膨壓力加劇，將迫使聯準會維持緊縮政策。高利率環境直接衝擊超大規模業者（Microsoft、Google）的資本支出決策，因數據中心建設的融資成本上升，可能延緩或分階段執行AI基礎設施投資。NVIDIA的GPU訂單能見度面臨不確定性，特別是對利率敏感的企業客戶可能削減採購預算。
ai.en: CPI at 333.979 with persistent upward momentum (+0.5% MoM) signals entrenched inflation, keeping Fed rates elevated. Higher cost of capital directly pressures hyperscaler AI capex—Microsoft and Google face increased financing costs for multi-billion dollar data center builds, potentially phasing or delaying projects. NVIDIA GPU order visibility weakens as rate-sensitive enterprise customers trim budgets. Sustained inflation above 2% target maintains restrictive monetary stance, dampening the aggressive infrastructure spending cycle critical to AI hardware demand.

mobile.zh: CPI連續12個月攀升至333.979反映消費者購買力持續受侵蝕，可支配所得縮減直接打擊非必需品消費。智慧型手機平均售價已超過1,000美元，高通膨環境下消費者延長換機週期，Apple與Samsung旗艦機種需求疲軟。零售商庫存去化緩慢，促銷力度加大壓縮供應鏈毛利。中低階機種市場競爭加劇，但整體出貨量年增率恐轉負。
mobile.en: CPI's climb to 333.979 erodes real disposable income, hitting discretionary spending hard. With flagship smartphones exceeding $1,000 ASP, consumers extend replacement cycles—Apple and Samsung face weakening premium tier demand. Inflation-squeezed budgets shift mix toward value segments, compressing margins across the supply chain. Retail inventory digestion slows, requiring deeper promotions. The +0.5% MoM increase signals no relief ahead; expect YoY shipment contraction as households prioritize essentials over device upgrades amid persistent price pressures.

auto.zh: CPI突破333持續通膨迫使車貸利率維持高檔（7%以上），每月還款額大幅增加抑制購車需求。傳統燃油車銷售疲軟，而EV市場面臨雙重打擊：高利率提升總持有成本，且補貼政策退場。經銷商庫存天數攀升至90天以上，被迫提供現金折扣侵蝕獲利。供應鏈端MCU與車用晶片需求下修，二線供應商面臨砍單壓力。
auto.en: CPI at 333.979 keeps auto loan rates above 7%, inflating monthly payments and crushing affordability—a $40,000 vehicle now costs $200+ more per month versus pre-inflation levels. ICE sales stumble while EVs face twin headwinds: high financing costs negating fuel savings and subsidy phaseouts. Dealer inventory swells past 90 days, forcing margin-eroding incentives. Supply chain feels ripple effects—MCU and automotive chip demand downgrades accelerate as OEMs cut production schedules. Persistent 0.5% monthly CPI gains offer no near-term financing relief.

tsmc.zh: CPI持續走高間接影響TSMC客戶組合：智慧型手機與車用晶片需求因消費疲軟而走弱，衝擊N7/N5成熟製程利用率。然而AI伺服器晶片（N3/N4）需求相對剛性，NVIDIA、AMD訂單支撐先進製程稼動率維持高檔。通膨推升設備與原物料成本，3nm擴產的單位資本支出上升，但客戶願意承擔溢價確保產能。整體而言先進製程撐住獲利，成熟製程面臨價格壓力。
tsmc.en: CPI at 333.979 creates divergent demand signals for TSMC: smartphone and automotive chip orders weaken as consumer spending falters, pressuring N7/N5 mature node utilization rates. Conversely, AI server processors on N3/N4 show resilient demand—NVIDIA and AMD prioritize capacity despite inflation, keeping leading-edge fab utilization elevated. Inflation raises capex per wafer as equipment and material costs climb, squeezing margins on mature nodes while premium customers absorb N3 price increases. Mixed outlook: advanced node strength offsets consumer-driven weakness, but sustained 0.5% monthly CPI gains threaten broader demand normalization.

commentary.zh: 核心CPI在5月份上升至336.121，月增0.2%，持續溫和上升趨勢。自2025年5月以來，累積漲幅約3.1%，顯示通膨壓力未見明顯消退。儘管月度增速保持溫和，但年增速仍維持在相對高位，暗示聯準會降息空間受限。未來若核心CPI持續徘徊高檔，將強化市場對升息延期或維持高利率更久的預期，對債券和股票市場均構成壓力。
commentary.en: Core CPI rose to 336.121 in May, up 0.2% month-over-month, extending the steady upward trajectory. Since May 2025, the index has climbed approximately 3.1%, indicating inflation pressures remain elevated. While monthly gains remain modest, the year-over-year pace stays relatively high, constraining Fed rate-cut prospects. Persistent sticky inflation at these levels would reinforce market expectations for prolonged higher rates, weighing on both bond and equity valuations.

ai.zh: 核心CPI升至336.121（月增0.2%），年化增幅持續高於聯準會目標，強化限制性利率政策預期。高利率環境壓縮超大規模業者的資本支出回報率，Microsoft、Google及Meta的數據中心擴張計畫面臨更高折現率考驗。NVIDIA GPU訂單能見度可能因客戶重新評估AI基礎設施ROI而趨謹慎，尤其是邊際報酬遞減的訓練集群投資。債務成本上升亦削弱AI新創企業的融資能力，減緩雲端運算需求成長動能。
ai.en: Core CPI reaching 336.121 (+0.2% MoM) sustains elevated inflation, reinforcing restrictive Fed policy. Higher discount rates erode hyperscaler capex returns—Microsoft, Google, and Meta face tougher justification for datacenter expansion. NVIDIA GPU order visibility may soften as customers reassess AI infrastructure ROI amid rising debt costs. Training cluster investments see diminishing marginal returns at higher rates. Elevated borrowing costs also constrain AI startup funding, slowing cloud compute demand growth. Persistent core inflation above Fed targets delays any pivot to accommodative policy that would benefit capital-intensive AI buildouts.

mobile.zh: 核心CPI月增0.2%至336.121，服務類通膨持續侵蝕消費者可支配所得，直接衝擊非必需品支出。智慧型手機平均售價已逾1,000美元，高通膨環境下換機週期延長至3.5年以上。Apple iPhone出貨面臨雙重壓力：利率不降抑制分期付款需求，實質薪資成長停滯削弱高階機種買氣。Samsung中階機型競爭加劇但利潤受壓。核心CPI居高暗示聯準會難快速降息，消費性電子產品庫存調整期恐延長至2026下半年。
mobile.en: Core CPI at 336.121 (+0.2% MoM) signals persistent services inflation eroding discretionary spending. Smartphone replacement cycles extend beyond 3.5 years as $1,000+ average prices clash with stagnant real wages. Apple iPhone shipments face dual headwinds: sustained high rates suppress installment plan uptake, while sticky inflation delays Fed rate cuts that would revive premium tier demand. Samsung's mid-range competition intensifies but margins suffer. Elevated core CPI keeps financing costs high—0% carrier promotions less viable. Consumer electronics inventory corrections likely persist into H2 2026 absent inflation deceleration.

auto.zh: 核心CPI達336.121持續推升借貸成本，72個月期汽車貸款利率維持7%以上，月付款負擔較2020年增加40%。傳統燃油車與EV銷售均受衝擊：高利率使每輛車融資成本增加3,000-4,000美元，抵銷IRA補貼效益。通用、福特的庫存天數攀升至80天以上。EV採用率成長停滯於9%，因消費者延後大額支出。核心通膨不降，聯準會難放鬆政策，車市復甦推遲至2027年。MCU及車用半導體需求疲軟，衝擊NXP、英飛凌訂單。
auto.en: Core CPI at 336.121 keeps auto loan rates above 7% for 72-month terms—monthly payments up 40% vs. 2020. Financing costs add $3,000-4,000 per vehicle, negating IRA EV credits. GM and Ford inventories swell beyond 80 days as affordability crunch deepens. EV adoption stalls at 9% penetration—consumers defer big-ticket purchases amid persistent inflation. Pickup and SUV demand especially soft as interest expense balloons on $60,000+ MSRPs. Sustained core inflation delays Fed easing, pushing auto market recovery to 2027. Weakened demand cascades to MCU and automotive semiconductor orders, pressuring NXP and Infineon revenues.

tsmc.zh: 核心CPI月增0.2%至336.121對台積電影響分歧。負面：智慧型手機及車用晶片需求疲軟，7nm/6nm產能利用率跌至70%，成熟製程營收佔比下滑。蘋果A系列及高通訂單因iPhone銷售放緩而減少15-20%。正面：AI加速器需求支撐3nm/2nm先進製程滿載，NVIDIA H200/B200及AMD MI300訂單強勁，CoWoS封裝產能供不應求延至2026 Q4。高通膨若迫使聯準會維持緊縮，客戶組合加速向高毛利AI晶片傾斜，但整體營收成長率仍受消費性電子拖累降至個位數。
tsmc.en: Core CPI at 336.121 (+0.2%) creates divergent impacts for TSMC. Negative: Smartphone and auto chip demand weakens—7nm/6nm utilization drops to 70% as Apple A-series and Qualcomm orders decline 15-20% on soft iPhone sales. Automotive MCU demand sags with prolonged vehicle downturn. Positive: AI accelerator demand keeps 3nm/2nm fabs at full capacity—NVIDIA H200/B200 and AMD MI300 orders robust, CoWoS packaging bottlenecks persist into Q4 2026. Persistent core inflation sustaining restrictive Fed policy accelerates customer mix shift toward high-margin AI silicon, but overall revenue growth moderates to single digits as consumer electronics drag offsets datacenter strength.

commentary.zh: 5月核心PCE達130.082，月增0.3%，延續溫和上升軌跡。自6月以來累計漲幅3.2%，月均增速約0.27%，顯示通膨壓力持續但未加劇。與聯準會2%長期目標相比仍有距離，惟近月加速態勢值得關注。若此趨勢延續，可能影響聯準會降息時程，市場應密切監控後續數據。
commentary.en: Core PCE reached 130.082 in May, up 0.3% monthly, maintaining a gradual uptrend. Since June, cumulative gains total 3.2% with average monthly increases around 0.27%, indicating persistent but not accelerating inflation. While above the Fed's 2% long-term target, the steady pace doesn't suggest immediate policy urgency. Continued monitoring is warranted, as sustained acceleration could delay rate cuts.

ai.zh: 核心 PCE 於五月達 130.082（月增 0.3%），持續高於聯準會 2% 目標，暗示利率將維持限制性水準更久。這對 NVIDIA、微軟等超大規模業者的 AI 資本支出形成壓力，因借貸成本居高不下。然而，企業為透過自動化對抗工資通膨，仍持續投資 GPU 與資料中心基礎設施，需求韌性支撐毛利率，但擴張步調可能趨緩。
ai.en: Core PCE at 130.082 (+0.3% MoM) remains well above the Fed's 2% target, signaling prolonged restrictive rates that pressure AI capex financing costs for hyperscalers like Microsoft and Google. However, persistent inflation—especially wage pressures—drives enterprises to invest in automation and GPU-based efficiency gains, sustaining demand for NVIDIA datacenter chips. The trajectory suggests resilient but decelerating expansion as higher capital costs weigh on new datacenter projects and lease economics.

mobile.zh: 核心 PCE 自去年六月的 126.121 穩步攀升至 130.082，累積漲幅 3.1%，侵蝕消費者可支配所得。高通膨環境下，消費者延長換機週期，對 Apple 與 Samsung 旗艦機種需求疲軟。聯準會維持高利率抑制通膨，進一步壓抑非必需消費電子支出。預期出貨量持平或下滑，平均售價成長受限，庫存調整壓力增加。
mobile.en: Core PCE rising from 126.121 (June 2025) to 130.082 represents 3.1% cumulative inflation, eroding real disposable income and extending smartphone replacement cycles. Consumers prioritize essentials over discretionary electronics, weakening demand for Apple and Samsung flagships. Persistent above-target inflation keeps Fed rates elevated, tightening consumer credit and dampening upgrade appetite. Expect flat-to-declining shipment volumes, limited ASP growth, and inventory correction pressures through Q3 2026.

auto.zh: 核心 PCE 月增 0.3% 至 130.082，強化聯準會鷹派立場，汽車貸款利率維持 7-8% 高檔。高融資成本直接打擊購車需求，尤其是價格較高的電動車款，衝擊 EV 滲透率成長。通膨持續侵蝕消費者購買力，新車銷售疲軟，經銷商庫存天數攀升。供應鏈雖已正常化，但需求端萎縮導致 MCU 與車用晶片訂單下修，產能利用率下滑。
auto.en: Core PCE at 130.082 (+0.3% MoM) reinforces Fed hawkishness, keeping auto loan rates near 7-8% and crushing affordability. Monthly payments on $40k vehicles rise ~$80 versus 2022, suppressing both ICE and EV sales—particularly hitting premium EV adoption. Sustained inflation erodes real wages, extending ownership cycles and lifting dealer inventory days. Weaker demand cascades to semiconductor content: lower pickup and EV volumes reduce MCU, ADAS chip orders, pressuring utilization at auto-focused fabs.

tsmc.zh: 核心 PCE 達 130.082 反映持續通膨壓力，高利率環境對消費性電子（手機、PC）晶片需求不利，衝擊 TSMC 的 N5/N4 產能利用率。然而，AI 伺服器晶片（NVIDIA H100/H200、AMD MI300）在先進製程需求強勁，部分抵銷消費端疲軟。客戶組合分化：超大規模業者持續下單 N3E/N3P，但 Apple iPhone 晶片訂單成長受限。資本支出展望審慎，聚焦高毛利 AI 產能。
tsmc.en: Core PCE at 130.082 (+0.3%) sustains elevated rates, weakening consumer electronics demand and pressuring TSMC's N5/N4 utilization for smartphone and PC chips—especially Apple A-series orders amid soft iPhone volumes. Conversely, AI datacenter chips (NVIDIA H100/B200, AMD MI300) on N3E/N3P nodes remain robust as hyperscalers prioritize automation despite financing costs. Customer mix bifurcates: AI revenue share rises while mobile/PC exposure drags. Capex outlook cautious, focusing N2 investments on high-margin AI workloads rather than broad consumer recovery.

commentary.zh: 5月非農就業人數達159,001千人，環比增加172千人（+0.1%），持續溫和成長。近12個月數據在158,400-159,000千人區間波動，顯示就業市場已進入平穩期，月均新增就業遠低於健康水準的150,000人門檻。儘管未見明顯衰退跡象，但成長動能明顯放緩，反映勞動力需求趨於謹慎。聯準會將密切關注此數據作為政策調整依據，若持續低於預期，可能支持降息預期。
commentary.en: May nonfarm payrolls reached 159.0 million, up 172,000 (+0.1%) from prior month. The labor market remains stable but growth is decelerating, with recent months clustered between 158.4–159.0 million. Monthly job creation has consistently fallen short of the 150,000+ threshold for robust expansion, signaling moderating labor demand. While no recessionary signals emerge, the trend reflects cautious hiring patterns. The Fed will monitor this closely; sustained weakness could reinforce expectations for rate cuts.

ai.zh: 5月新增17.2萬非農就業，連續兩月回升且超過15萬健康門檻，顯示勞動市場穩健。強勁就業支撐企業獲利預期，促使超大規模雲端業者（Microsoft、Google、Meta）維持積極AI基礎建設投資。數據中心擴建與GPU採購週期不受干擾，NVIDIA H100/B200訂單能見度提升。就業增長亦降低聯準會近期降息壓力，但尚未觸發緊縮，為AI資本支出提供穩定融資環境。
ai.en: May's +172K payroll gain, second consecutive month above the 150K health threshold, signals robust labor market momentum. Strong employment bolsters corporate profit expectations, sustaining hyperscaler AI capex commitments from Microsoft, Google, and Meta for data center builds and GPU procurement. NVIDIA's H100/B200 order visibility improves as enterprise IT budgets remain well-funded. The steady job growth reduces immediate Fed rate-cut pressure but avoids triggering tightening, maintaining favorable financing conditions for multi-billion AI infrastructure investments through 2026.

mobile.zh: 5月非農就業增17.2萬人，但總就業僅較去年同期成長0.3%，增速疲弱。雖月增數據健康，年增率停滯暗示消費者可支配所得成長受限，制約高階手機（iPhone Pro、Galaxy S系列）換機需求。蘋果與三星出貨面臨「就業穩定但薪資動能不足」困境，中階機種競爭加劇。聯準會維持觀望，利率高檔持續，分期付款成本仍偏高，5G換機週期延長至36個月以上。
mobile.en: May's +172K addition appears healthy, but year-over-year employment growth stalls at just +0.3%, signaling weak momentum in disposable income expansion. This constrains premium smartphone replacement cycles for iPhone Pro and Galaxy S flagships, as consumers delay upgrades despite stable jobs. Apple and Samsung face a 'steady employment, weak wage growth' paradox, intensifying mid-tier competition. Fed maintains restrictive rates, keeping installment financing costs elevated and stretching 5G replacement cycles beyond 36 months, dampening second-half shipment forecasts.

auto.zh: 5月就業增17.2萬，支撐汽車貸款信用品質，但年增率僅0.3%反映薪資成長動能不足。強勁月增數據降低經濟衰退風險，穩定消費者購車信心，有利中階燃油車與入門EV銷售。然而就業增長未促使聯準會降息，車貸利率維持6.5%以上高檔，抑制高價皮卡與豪華電動車（F-150 Lightning、Rivian）需求。經銷商庫存天數回升至70天，促銷壓力增加，利潤率承壓。
auto.en: May's +172K gain supports auto loan credit quality and reduces recession risk, stabilizing consumer sentiment for mid-tier ICE and entry EV purchases. However, year-over-year growth of only +0.3% indicates sluggish wage momentum, while steady employment keeps the Fed on hold with auto loan rates above 6.5%. This bifurcates demand: reliable job security aids mainstream sedan and compact SUV sales, but elevated financing costs suppress premium pickup (F-150 Lightning) and luxury EV uptake. Dealer inventories climb to 70-day supply, pressuring margins through increased incentives.

tsmc.zh: 5月非農就業連續增長且超越15萬門檻，確保AI伺服器與高效能運算終端需求穩健，直接帶動台積電3奈米（N3E）與5奈米先進製程投片量。NVIDIA、AMD、博通等HPC客戶資本支出不縮減，CoWoS先進封裝產能利用率維持滿載。雖消費性手機晶片需求平淡，但企業級AI加速器、數據中心CPU訂單彌補缺口。穩定就業降低客戶砍單風險，支撐台積電2026年資本支出維持280-300億美元高檔計畫。
tsmc.en: May's +172K payroll addition, exceeding the 150K threshold for two consecutive months, underpins robust AI server and HPC endpoint demand, driving TSMC's 3nm (N3E) and 5nm advanced node wafer starts. NVIDIA, AMD, and Broadcom sustain elevated capex, keeping CoWoS advanced packaging at full utilization. While consumer smartphone chip demand remains tepid due to weak year-over-year employment growth (+0.3%), enterprise AI accelerator and datacenter CPU orders fill the gap. Stable labor markets reduce customer order cancellation risk, supporting TSMC's $28-30B capex plan through 2026 without cuts.

commentary.zh: 5月失業率維持在4.3%，與前月持平。過去一年數據在4.1%-4.5%區間波動，整體呈現穩定略升趨勢。雖仍接近充分就業目標，但較2025年中期的4.1%低點已上升0.2個百分點，反映勞動市場冷卻跡象。聯準會面臨通膨與就業的平衡抉擇，失業率溫和上升可能支持進一步降息空間，惟需密切觀察後續就業數據是否持續惡化。
commentary.en: May unemployment held steady at 4.3%, unchanged from April. Over the past year, readings have fluctuated between 4.1% and 4.5%, showing mild upward drift from the 4.1% trough in mid-2025. While still near full employment targets, the 0.2 percentage point increase signals moderate labor market softening. This trajectory may support Fed rate cuts if softness continues, though policymakers will monitor whether weakness accelerates. Current stability suggests labor market remains reasonably resilient despite gradual cooling.

ai.zh: 失業率維持4.3%且過去一年在4.1-4.5%窄幅波動,顯示勞動市場穩健但非過熱。此環境支持聯準會維持中性利率政策,降低hyperscaler資金成本壓力。穩定就業支撐企業IT支出信心,Microsoft、Google等持續擴張資料中心投資。NVIDIA GPU需求維持強勁,因企業在經濟穩定期更願進行長期AI基礎建設。唯一風險是若失業率突破4.5%可能削弱企業capex意願。
ai.en: Unemployment stable at 4.3%, oscillating narrowly between 4.1-4.5% over twelve months, signals healthy labor market without overheating. This supports Fed neutrality, easing financing costs for hyperscaler capex. Steady employment underpins corporate IT budgets—Microsoft, Google, AWS maintain aggressive data center buildouts. NVIDIA GPU demand remains robust as stable macro conditions encourage multi-year AI infrastructure commitments. The 4.3% level sits near full employment, sustaining business confidence for continued AI capital deployment without recession fears that would curtail spending.

mobile.zh: 失業率4.3%持平且近期在4.1-4.5%區間徘徊,反映消費者就業與收入穩定但缺乏上行動能。智慧型手機屬可選消費,當前水準支持換機需求但不足以推動超級週期。Apple與Samsung出貨量維持平穩,高階機種銷售尚可但中低階市場競爭激烈。失業率未惡化避免需求崩跌,但也未改善至3.5%以刺激消費者信心大幅提升,整體手機市場呈現溫和持平格局。
mobile.en: Unemployment flat at 4.3%, ranging 4.1-4.5% recently, indicates stable consumer income but lacks upward momentum. Smartphone demand—a discretionary category—remains steady without acceleration. Apple and Samsung shipments hold baseline replacement cycles; premium iPhone and Galaxy S sales supported by employed consumers, but mid-tier segments face pricing pressure. The 4.3% level prevents demand collapse yet insufficient to drive confidence for aggressive upgrades. Absence of labor market improvement toward 3.5% means consumer electronics remain in holding pattern, neither contracting nor expanding meaningfully.

auto.zh: 失業率4.3%未變且維持接近充分就業,理論上支持汽車消費能力,但近一年未見明顯下降趨勢限制需求上行空間。穩定就業維持基本購車需求與貸款核准率,Ford、GM銷量持平。然而4.3%未降至3.5%意味薪資壓力有限,加上車貸利率仍偏高,抑制pick-up與SUV等高價車款銷售。EV採用受政策影響更甚於就業率。供應鏈穩定但終端需求動能不足,經銷商庫存微升。
auto.en: Unemployment steady at 4.3%, unchanged and hovering near full employment, supports baseline auto affordability but lacks downward trend to boost demand. Stable jobless rate sustains loan approval rates and entry-level vehicle sales for Ford, GM, and Toyota. However, persistence at 4.3% versus decline toward 3.5% means wage growth remains modest; combined with elevated auto loan rates (6-7%), this crimps pickup and SUV demand. EV adoption driven more by policy than employment. Supply chains stable, yet dealership inventories creep higher as purchasing power stagnates without labor market tightening.

tsmc.zh: 失業率4.3%持平,勞動市場穩定支持TSMC主要客戶(Apple、NVIDIA、AMD)維持晶片訂單但未見加速。智慧型手機需求平穩轉化為N3先進製程穩定利用率,但非滿載。AI伺服器需求強勁支撐CoWoS產能,抵銷消費電子疲軟。失業率未惡化確保客戶財務健康與capex持續,但4.3%水準未能推動終端需求顯著成長,TSMC整體晶圓需求呈結構性分化:HPC強、手機與車用平穩,先進製程利用率維持80-85%。
tsmc.en: Unemployment flat at 4.3% supports steady but not accelerating wafer demand for TSMC. Stable labor market underpins customer health—Apple orders for iPhone chips hold at N3 node utilization around 80-85%, while NVIDIA and AMD AI GPU demand sustains CoWoS advanced packaging at near capacity. Smartphone stagnation limits A-series processor volume growth; automotive chip orders stable as vehicle production plateaus. The 4.3% level ensures hyperscaler capex continuity without recession risk, but absence of improvement toward 3.5% means no end-demand surge. TSMC sees structural bifurcation: HPC/AI robust, consumer electronics flat, blended utilization steady without near-term expansion trigger.

commentary.zh: 最新數據為21.5萬人，較前週下降1.2萬人(-5.3%)，顯示勞動市場穩定跡象。四週移動平均約21.5萬，遠低於30萬警戒線，表明就業市場維持韌性。不過6月初曾升至23萬，近期有所回落。當前水位仍屬健康範圍，但市場密切關注是否形成上升趨勢。若未來數週持續攀升，恐觸發聯準會對勞動市場轉弱的憂慮，影響降息時程。
commentary.en: Initial claims fell to 215,000 from 227,000 last week, a 5.3% decline signaling labor market resilience. The four-week average remains well below the 300,000 threshold that signals deterioration. However, claims spiked to 230,000 in early June before moderating. Current levels suggest healthy labor conditions, though the recent volatility warrants monitoring. Sustained upward pressure could prompt Fed concern about labor softening, potentially delaying rate cuts.

ai.zh: 初次申請失業救濟金人數降至21.5萬，較前週下降1.2萬人(-5.3%)，但近期在19萬至23萬間波動。由於AI基礎建設投資主要由企業資本支出驅動而非消費需求，且超大規模業者(Microsoft、Google)的資料中心建設與GPU採購計劃通常跨多年期，短期勞動市場波動對NVIDIA H100/B100訂單影響有限。若失業數據持續惡化突破30萬門檻，可能促使企業重新評估2027年AI capex預算。
ai.en: Claims fell to 215K (-12K, -5.3%), oscillating between 190K-230K recently. AI infrastructure spending by hyperscalers (Microsoft, Google, Meta) is driven by multi-year datacenter buildouts and enterprise capex, not consumer demand. NVIDIA GPU orders and H100/B100 deployment schedules remain insulated from weekly labor volatility. However, sustained deterioration above 300K could trigger corporate budget reassessments for 2027 AI capex, potentially delaying next-gen cluster expansions and impacting server-side demand.

mobile.zh: 失業救濟金申請人數從23萬降至21.5萬，顯示勞動市場維持韌性但未明顯改善。近三個月數據在19萬至23萬區間震盪，尚未觸及30萬警戒線。消費者對iPhone 16及Samsung Galaxy旗艦機種的可支配支出暫時穩定，但換機週期已延長至3.5年以上。若失業數據回升並持續超過22萬，將進一步壓抑非必需消費電子支出，影響Apple在北美的ASP(平均售價)及高階機種mix，中低階Android市佔可能相對受益。
mobile.en: Claims dropped to 215K from 230K (-5.3%), hovering in the 190K-230K range without breaching the 300K threshold. Labor resilience supports discretionary spending on flagship devices (iPhone 16, Galaxy S26), though replacement cycles have stretched beyond 3.5 years. Smartphone demand remains price-sensitive; sustained claims above 220K would pressure Apple's North American ASP and shift mix toward mid-tier Android. Current levels suggest stable but uninspiring demand—no catalyst for accelerated upgrades or premium tier growth in consumer electronics.

auto.zh: 失業人數降至21.5萬(-5.3%)屬正面訊號，但6月初曾升至23萬，反映勞動市場不穩定。汽車購買高度依賴就業信心與貸款可得性，目前平均新車貸款利率仍在7%以上，月付款壓力限制需求。失業數據改善有助穩定消費者信心，支撐pickup truck及SUV銷售，但若數據反彈超過22萬，將直接衝擊次級信用客戶的融資批准率，減緩Ford F-150、Silverado等高利潤車款銷量。EV採購因價格敏感度更高，受失業波動影響更顯著。
auto.en: Claims fell to 215K (-12K), recovering from early June's 230K spike, but volatility signals labor market fragility. Auto purchases depend heavily on employment confidence and financing—average loan rates above 7% already constrain affordability. The decline supports near-term pickup/SUV sales (F-150, Silverado) and stabilizes subprime approval rates. However, if claims reverse above 220K, financing tightens further, hitting high-margin truck demand and delaying EV adoption among cost-sensitive buyers. Inventory financing costs remain elevated, pressuring dealer margins and promotional activity.

tsmc.zh: 失業人數降至21.5萬，勞動市場穩定但未強勁復甦。TSMC面臨差異化影響：AI相關3nm/5nm晶圓需求(NVIDIA H100/B200、AMD MI300)受企業資本支出驅動，不受短期就業數據干擾，先進製程利用率維持高檔。然而智慧型手機及PC相關7nm/6nm訂單與消費者信心連動，若失業數據持續在21-23萬徘徊，Apple A18、Qualcomm驍龍客戶端訂單恐難有超預期成長。整體而言，CoWoS先進封裝需求支撐營收，但消費端疲弱限制產能擴張urgency。
tsmc.en: Claims at 215K (-5.3%) show labor stability without strong recovery. TSMC faces bifurcated demand: 3nm/5nm wafer orders for AI accelerators (NVIDIA B200, AMD MI300X) remain robust, driven by hyperscaler capex insensitive to weekly employment swings—advanced node utilization stays elevated. Conversely, 7nm/6nm demand tied to smartphones (Apple A18, Qualcomm) and PCs correlates with consumer confidence; claims hovering at 215-225K suggest muted upside for client-facing orders. CoWoS packaging for AI chips supports revenue mix, but consumer weakness tempers urgency for aggressive N2 node capex expansion through 2027.

commentary.zh: 美國實質GDP在2026年首季達24.18兆美元，季增0.5%，延續溫和成長軌跡。自2023年第二季以來累計成長約7.1%，年化成長率約1.7-2%，略低於健康區間。值得注意的是，2025年初出現小幅回落後，近兩季恢復增長勢頭。經濟仍避免衰退風險，但成長動能溫和，可能支持聯準會維持較寬鬆立場，同時密切監測通膨與勞動市場表現。
commentary.en: Real GDP reached $24.18 trillion in Q1 2026, posting a modest 0.5% quarterly gain. Since Q2 2023, cumulative growth totals 7.1%, translating to approximately 1.7-2% annualized growth—slightly below the healthy 2-3% benchmark. A dip in early 2025 reversed course with renewed gains last two quarters, keeping recession risk at bay. Growth momentum remains subdued, likely supporting Federal Reserve patience on rate cuts while monitoring inflation and labor market conditions closely.

ai.zh: 實質GDP達24,180億美元，季增125億（+0.5%），年化成長率約2%，符合健康擴張水平。穩定的經濟成長支撐企業IT支出預算，特別是雲端hyperscaler（微軟、Google）持續投資AI基礎設施。GDP擴張帶動企業數位轉型需求，推升資料中心建設與GPU採購。NVIDIA的H100/H200需求維持強勁，企業在經濟穩定環境下更願意進行長期AI資本支出，訓練與推論工作負載持續擴增。
ai.en: GDP at $24,180B (+$125B, +0.5% QoQ) signals ~2% annualized growth, supporting robust enterprise IT budgets. Stable expansion encourages hyperscalers (Microsoft, Google, Amazon) to maintain aggressive AI capex cycles for data center builds. Healthy GDP growth correlates with corporate digital transformation spend, driving sustained GPU demand (NVIDIA H100/H200) and inference infrastructure deployment. Economic stability reduces budget freeze risks, keeping AI training workloads and cloud infrastructure investment on track through 2026.

mobile.zh: GDP季增0.5%顯示經濟溫和成長，但增速放緩（前季成長較高）。手機市場已高度飽和，2-3%的GDP成長不足以顯著刺激換機需求。消費者在經濟不確定性下延長換機週期至3-4年，iPhone與三星旗艦機銷售持平。中國市場疲軟及通膨壓力使消費者轉向中階機型。整體出貨量預計持平至微幅下滑，高階機種ASP面臨壓力，AI手機功能尚未成為強勁換機驅動力。
mobile.en: GDP growth of +0.5% QoQ reflects modest expansion but slowing momentum from prior quarters. Smartphone markets remain saturated; 2% GDP growth insufficient to trigger upgrade cycles now extended to 3-4 years. Consumer discretionary spending cautious amid lingering inflation memories—iPhone and Samsung flagship volumes flat. China weakness persists, pushing mix toward mid-tier devices. Shipment outlook neutral to slightly negative; AI smartphone features not yet compelling enough to drive replacement despite stable macro backdrop. ASP pressure continues.

auto.zh: GDP增長0.5%提供溫和支撐，但汽車業面臨結構性挑戰。雖經濟穩定有助就業與消費信心，但高利率環境使汽車貸款成本居高不下（平均APR達7-8%），抑制皮卡與SUV等高價車型需求。EV採購因補貼政策不確定性而放緩，消費者觀望。庫存正常化後，供應鏈壓力緩解但定價權下降。整體銷量年化約1,550萬輛，持平但未見強勁反彈，MCU與車用半導體需求成長有限。
auto.en: GDP +0.5% provides modest tailwind, but auto sector faces structural headwinds. Stable GDP supports employment and consumer confidence, yet elevated interest rates (7-8% APR on auto loans) dampen demand for pickups and SUVs. EV adoption slowing amid subsidy uncertainty and range anxiety; consumers delaying purchases. Inventory normalization complete, reducing dealer urgency. SAAR tracking ~15.5M units—flat, not rebounding. Lower financing affordability limits premium trim uptake, capping MCU and semiconductor content growth despite stable macro.

tsmc.zh: GDP達24,180億美元且穩定成長，直接利好台積電。AI晶片需求（NVIDIA H100/H200、AMD MI300）推動N3/N4先進製程滿載，利用率超過95%。雖手機與汽車需求溫和，但hyperscaler的AI accelerator訂單彌補消費性電子疲軟。美國經濟穩定使客戶資本支出信心增強，支撐台積電2026年資本支出維持250-280億美元高位。CoWoS先進封裝產能擴充持續，客戶組合向高毛利AI晶片傾斜，營收與毛利率展望正面。
tsmc.en: GDP at $24,180B with steady growth strongly favors TSMC. AI chip demand (NVIDIA, AMD, Broadcom) drives N3/N4 advanced node utilization above 95%, offsetting softer smartphone and auto chip demand. Hyperscaler AI accelerator orders sustain wafer starts; stable US GDP reinforces customer capex confidence, supporting TSMC's $25-28B capex guidance through 2026. CoWoS advanced packaging capacity expansions continue as customer mix shifts toward high-margin AI processors. Revenue and gross margin outlook positive despite mixed end-market signals elsewhere.

commentary.zh: 5月零售銷售（不含汽車）達6627.52億美元，環比成長1.0%，延續年初以來的上升軌跡。自1月以來累計成長4.6%，反映消費支出持續穩健。近月數據顯示消費動能加速—3月至5月的三月平均成長率約1.3%，優於前期。考量消費支出佔GDP約70%，此強勁表現印證經濟韌性，有助支撐經濟成長預期。若此勢頭維持，可能減輕聯準會進一步降息的迫切性。
commentary.en: May retail sales (ex-autos) reached $662.8B, up 1.0% month-over-month, continuing the uptrend since January. Cumulative growth of 4.6% over five months signals robust consumer spending momentum. Notably, the March-May period shows accelerating gains (~1.3% average monthly), outpacing earlier months. Given consumption's 70% GDP weight, this strength underscores economic resilience and reduces pressure for aggressive Fed rate cuts. Market expectations may shift toward a more hawkish stance if momentum persists.

ai.zh: 零售銷售額達 6,628 億美元，月增 1.0%，反映消費者支出強勁，支撐企業獲利能力。健康的消費環境使電商、串流、雲端服務需求持續擴張，推動 Microsoft、Google、Amazon 等超大規模業者加碼 AI 資本支出。強勁零售數據確認企業端有現金流投資生成式 AI 基礎設施，利好 NVIDIA GPU 訂單與資料中心擴建週期。
ai.en: Retail sales at $662.8B (+1.0% MoM) signal robust consumer spending, strengthening corporate revenues for e-commerce, streaming, and cloud services. This healthy consumption backdrop validates continued AI capex by hyperscalers—Microsoft, Google, Amazon—as enterprise cash flows remain strong. Rising digital service demand directly fuels datacenter expansion and GPU procurement from NVIDIA, sustaining the AI infrastructure buildout cycle through at least mid-2026.

mobile.zh: 零售銷售月增 1.0% 至 6,628 億美元，且自 2 月起連續四個月加速成長（2 月 +0.95%、3 月 +1.99%、4 月 +0.33%、5 月 +1.0%），顯示消費者可支配所得充裕。這利好 Apple iPhone 與 Samsung 旗艦機需求，尤其高階機型與 AI 功能手機。電子產品零售復甦支撐智慧型手機換機週期延續，有助出貨量與平均售價同步提升。
mobile.en: Retail sales rose 1.0% to $662.8B, with four consecutive months of growth since February, indicating solid discretionary income. This supports premium smartphone demand—particularly Apple iPhone and Samsung flagships with AI features. Strong consumer electronics spending underpins upgrade cycles, driving both shipment volume and ASP expansion. The sustained retail momentum reduces inventory risk and validates OEM production ramps into Q3 2026.

auto.zh: 本次數據剔除汽車銷售，無法直接評估車市。但零售銷售（不含汽車）月增 1.0% 顯示非汽車消費強勁，可能排擠汽車購買預算，尤其在利率仍高、車貸成本偏貴的環境下。消費者優先支出於日常零售與服務，對汽車與電動車需求形成競爭壓力，短期對 Ford、GM 銷量增長構成挑戰。
auto.en: This indicator excludes auto sales, limiting direct insight. However, the 1.0% MoM rise in non-auto retail to $662.8B suggests consumers prioritize everyday spending over big-ticket auto purchases, especially given elevated financing costs. Strong retail spending may divert wallet share away from vehicle purchases, pressuring Ford and GM volumes. EV adoption faces headwinds as discretionary budgets tighten for large durable goods amid sustained interest rate levels.

tsmc.zh: 零售銷售連續四個月成長至 6,628 億美元，反映終端消費需求健康，直接帶動智慧型手機、AI 伺服器、PC 等終端裝置出貨。Apple、NVIDIA、AMD 等 TSMC 主要客戶訂單能見度提升，3nm/5nm 先進製程產能利用率維持高檔。強勁消費數據支持客戶端晶片庫存回補與新品投片，鞏固 TSMC 下半年營收與資本支出展望。
tsmc.en: Retail sales at $662.8B with four-month growth streak confirms healthy end-demand for smartphones, AI servers, and PCs—directly benefiting TSMC's major customers (Apple, NVIDIA, AMD). This sustains high utilization at 3nm/5nm advanced nodes and validates customer chip inventory restocking and new tapeouts. Strong consumption data underpins TSMC's H2 2026 revenue visibility and supports ongoing capex in leading-edge capacity, with customer mix tilting toward high-margin AI and premium mobile processors.

commentary.zh: 聯邦基金利率於6月維持在3.63%，與5月持平。自2025年7月高點4.33%以來，聯準會已累計降息69個基點。自2026年1月起，利率連續五個月保持穩定在3.63-3.64%區間，反映聯準會採取暫停態勢。這表明政策制定者認為當前利率水準適當，同時觀察通膨與就業數據。市場應密切關注下次會議前的經濟數據，特別是物價與勞動力市場指標，以判斷聯準會是否將進一步調整政策立場。
commentary.en: The Federal Funds Rate held steady at 3.63% in June, unchanged from May. Since peaking at 4.33% in July 2025, the Fed has cut rates by 69 basis points. The rate has remained stable within the 3.63-3.64% band for five consecutive months since January 2026, signaling a pause in policy adjustments. This suggests Fed officials view current conditions as appropriately accommodative while monitoring inflation and employment data. Markets should watch upcoming economic releases closely—particularly inflation and labor metrics—to assess whether further policy adjustments lie ahead.

ai.zh: 聯邦基金利率自7月的4.33%降至目前3.63%，累計降息70個基點後近四個月穩定不變。低利率環境降低超大規模雲端業者（微軟、Google、Meta）的資本成本，使其AI資料中心建設與GPU採購的NPV更具吸引力。NVIDIA等供應商受惠於持續強勁的H100/H200訂單，儘管利率暫停下降，但3.63%仍處歷史低檔，支撐長週期AI基礎設施投資維持高檔。
ai.en: The Fed funds rate has declined 70 bps from 4.33% in July to 3.63%, stabilizing over the past four months. This lower cost of capital enhances NPV calculations for hyperscaler AI capex—Microsoft, Google, and Meta continue aggressive data center builds and GPU procurement. NVIDIA benefits from sustained H100/H200 demand as 3.63% remains historically accommodative, supporting multi-year infrastructure investments even as the rate cuts pause.

mobile.zh: 利率從4.33%降至3.63%後維持平穩，對手機需求的刺激效果有限。智慧型手機多為現金交易或短期分期，對利率敏感度低於汽車或房地產。蘋果與三星的換機週期主要受產品創新（如AI功能）而非融資成本驅動。3.63%的穩定利率提供溫和的消費者信心背景，但不足以扭轉成熟市場的換機延長趨勢或顯著提振出貨量。
mobile.en: The rate stabilizing at 3.63% after a 70 bps decline offers limited stimulus to smartphone demand. Mobile purchases are predominantly cash or short-term financing, less rate-sensitive than autos or housing. Apple and Samsung upgrade cycles hinge on product innovation (AI features, camera advances) rather than borrowing costs. While 3.63% supports baseline consumer sentiment, it won't reverse mature-market replacement elongation or materially boost shipment volumes.

auto.zh: 利率從4.33%降至3.63%顯著降低汽車貸款成本，五年期新車貸款月付金減少約5-6%，直接刺激購車需求。電動車銷售特別受惠，因EV平均單價較高對融資敏感度更強。經銷商庫存周轉加速，皮卡與SUV需求回升帶動更多MCU與感測器用量。若利率維持3.63%不再升息，2026下半年銷售年率有望突破1,600萬輛，供應鏈稼動率改善。
auto.en: The 70 bps decline to 3.63% materially lowers auto loan costs—monthly payments on a five-year new-car loan drop ~5-6%, directly stimulating sales. EV adoption accelerates as higher-priced electric models are more financing-sensitive. Dealer inventory turns improve, pickup and SUV demand rebounds, driving higher MCU and sensor content per vehicle. If rates hold at 3.63%, 2H 2026 SAAR could exceed 16 million units, improving supply chain utilization.

tsmc.zh: 利率降至3.63%後穩定，對台積電影響呈現分化。正面：低利率支撐超大規模客戶（微軟、Google）持續下單3nm/5nm AI晶片，CoWoS先進封裝產能維持滿載。負面：手機需求對利率不敏感，蘋果A系列與高通驍龍訂單僅溫和成長。汽車客戶受惠融資成本下降增加7nm/16nm車用晶片需求。整體而言，3.63%的環境支撐先進製程利用率維持85%以上，但不足以推動新一波大規模擴產。
tsmc.en: The rate at 3.63% creates mixed effects for TSMC. Positive: lower capital costs sustain hyperscaler orders for 3nm/5nm AI chips (Microsoft, Google), keeping CoWoS packaging at full utilization. Negative: smartphone demand remains rate-insensitive—Apple A-series and Qualcomm Snapdragon orders show only modest growth. Automotive customers benefit from cheaper financing, boosting 7nm/16nm auto chip demand. Net effect: 3.63% supports advanced node utilization above 85% but insufficient to trigger aggressive new capex expansion.

commentary.zh: 30年期房貸利率報6.49%，較前周小幅上升0.02%。近八周內在6.23%-6.53%區間波動，整體呈現上升壓力。自4月底低點6.3%以來，利率已上揚19個基點，反映市場對通膨前景的關注。雖未突破7%心理關卡，但持續高於6.4%水平將進一步侵蝕購房可負擔性，可能對住房成交量造成負面影響。後續走勢將取決於10年期美債殖利率與聯準會政策預期的演變。
commentary.en: 30-year mortgage rates edged up to 6.49%, marking a modest +2 basis point increase. Rates have fluctuated between 6.23%-6.53% over the past eight weeks, with an uptrend evident since late April's 6.30% low—a 19 basis point increase. While below the 7% threshold that severely constrains affordability, sustained levels above 6.40% continue eroding home purchase demand. Housing market weakness may persist if rates remain elevated. Future direction hinges on 10-year Treasury yields and Fed rate expectations.

ai.zh: 房貸利率維持在 6.49% 的溫和區間，對 AI 產業資本支出影響有限。超大規模業者如 Microsoft、Google 的數據中心投資主要受企業現金流與 AI 競爭驅動，而非住宅房貸成本。利率未突破 7% 關鍵壓力點，企業債成本仍可控，NVIDIA GPU 需求持續由生成式 AI 浪潮支撐。近期利率自 6.23% 反彈至 6.49%，顯示融資環境略緊但不足以改變 AI 基礎建設擴張週期。
ai.en: The 6.49% mortgage rate remains in a moderate range with minimal impact on AI capex. Hyperscaler investments by Microsoft, Google, and AWS are driven by cash flow and AI competition, not residential borrowing costs. Corporate debt remains manageable below the 7% stress threshold. NVIDIA GPU demand continues propelled by generative AI momentum. The recent climb from 6.23% to 6.49% signals modest tightening but insufficient to derail data center buildouts, as enterprise financing conditions remain supportive for infrastructure expansion.

mobile.zh: 房貸利率自 4 月低點 6.23% 攀升至 6.49%，壓縮美國家庭可支配收入，直接削弱非必需消費電子支出。iPhone 與 Samsung 旗艦機種平均單價超過 $1,000，在房貸月付增加下換機週期延長。利率連續七週維持 6.4% 以上，反映融資成本居高不下，消費者優先支付房貸而延後手機升級。Apple 北美市場佔營收 40%，高利率環境將拖累 Q3 出貨量，尤其衝擊高階機型需求。
mobile.en: Mortgage rates climbing from 6.23% in late April to 6.49% squeeze US household discretionary income, directly weakening consumer electronics spending. With iPhone and Samsung flagships averaging over $1,000, rising monthly mortgage payments extend replacement cycles. Seven consecutive weeks above 6.4% reflect persistent financing pressure, as consumers prioritize housing costs over phone upgrades. Apple derives 40% of revenue from North America, making high-rate environments a headwind for Q3 shipments, especially premium models facing affordability constraints.

auto.zh: 房貸利率 6.49% 與汽車貸款利率高度連動，目前新車貸款利率約 7-8%，月付款壓力顯著抑制購車需求。利率自 6.23% 反彈 26 基點，使 $40,000 新車的五年期貸款月付增加約 $15-20，直接衝擊中階轎車與電動車銷售。EV 平均售價較燃油車高 $10,000，融資成本上升使 Tesla、Ford 電動皮卡訂單轉換率下滑。供應鏈端，經銷商庫存天數增加將導致車用 MCU 與 ADAS 晶片需求遞延至 Q4。
auto.en: The 6.49% mortgage rate correlates with auto loan rates now at 7-8%, significantly pressuring purchase affordability. The 26-basis-point climb from 6.23% adds roughly $15-20 to monthly payments on a $40,000 five-year loan, directly hitting mid-tier sedan and EV sales. With EVs averaging $10,000 premiums over ICE vehicles, rising financing costs reduce conversion rates for Tesla and Ford electric trucks. On the supply side, rising dealer inventory days will defer demand for automotive MCUs and ADAS chips into Q4 as sales velocity slows.

tsmc.zh: 房貸利率 6.49% 對台積電呈現分化影響。消費端壓力將抑制 Apple A 系列與高通手機晶片 5nm/3nm 投片量，北美佔 iPhone 出貨 30% 面臨需求降溫。汽車市場疲軟同步影響 16nm/28nm 車用晶片稼動率。然而 AI 伺服器晶片（NVIDIA H100/H200）需求不受房貸利率干擾，CoWoS 先進封裝產能滿載至 2026 年底。整體而言，3nm 製程稼動率維持 80% 以上，但消費電子弱勢將使客戶組合更依賴 AI 與 HPC。
tsmc.en: The 6.49% rate creates mixed effects for TSMC. Consumer pressure will dampen Apple A-series and Qualcomm smartphone chip orders on 5nm/3nm nodes, with North America representing 30% of iPhone shipments facing cooling demand. Weak auto sales likewise soften 16nm/28nm automotive chip utilization. However, AI server chips (NVIDIA H100/H200) remain insulated from mortgage rates, with CoWoS advanced packaging fully booked through late 2026. Overall, 3nm utilization holds above 80%, but consumer electronics weakness shifts customer mix further toward AI and HPC dependencies.

commentary.zh: 5月製造業就業人數達12,605千人，環比小幅增長0.1%，扭轉年初至12月連續八個月的下滑態勢。但從2025年6月的12,636千人高點來看，過去一年仍累計下降31千人（-0.2%），反映出商品生產部門的疲弱。近三個月出現溫和反彈跡象，惟年增率持續負值仍為衰退風險的警訊，值得聯準會在評估勞動力市場強度時密切關注。
commentary.en: Manufacturing employment edged up to 12,605k in May (+0.1%), halting an eight-month decline from December 2025. However, it remains 31k below June 2025's peak (-0.2% year-over-year), signaling persistent goods-sector weakness. Recent three-month stabilization is modest; sustained negative year-over-year growth typically precedes broader recessions. This weakness warrants Fed attention when assessing labor market resilience, particularly if manufacturing contraction accelerates further.

ai.zh: 製造業就業人數從12,580千人谷底回升至12,605千人（+25千人），但仍低於半年前的12,636千人，顯示製造業復甦力道疲弱。對AI基礎設施而言，製造業就業與工業電力需求、B2B資本支出相關性高。當前就業數據顯示製造業產能利用率未明顯擴張，意味著超大規模業者的數據中心建設雖持續，但傳統製造業客戶對AI工具的企業級採購需求仍處觀望，限制AI應用層的商業化速度。
ai.en: Manufacturing employment recovered modestly from the December trough of 12,580K to 12,605K (+25K over five months), yet remains below the June peak of 12,636K, signaling weak industrial momentum. For AI infrastructure providers like NVIDIA and hyperscalers, this flat trajectory suggests subdued B2B capex from traditional manufacturing clients. While data center GPU procurement continues among cloud giants, the stagnant manufacturing job base indicates limited enterprise adoption of AI tooling in production environments, constraining commercial AI deployment beyond tech-native sectors and delaying broader AI capex acceleration.

mobile.zh: 製造業就業微增7千人至12,605千人，但較去年同期下降趨勢延續近一年，顯示藍領薪資購買力持續承壓。製造業工人為中階智慧型手機（$400-700區間）的核心消費群體，就業停滯直接削弱換機需求。Apple在美國市場的iPhone出貨將面對消費降級壓力，消費者延長換機週期或轉向翻新機，Samsung中階Galaxy系列同樣受創。預期北美智慧型手機ASP下滑，ODM訂單能見度維持保守。
mobile.en: Manufacturing employment added just 7K to 12,605K in May, extending a year-long downtrend from 12,636K in June 2025—a cumulative decline signaling sustained pressure on blue-collar purchasing power. Manufacturing workers form the core mid-tier smartphone market ($400-700 segment); job stagnation directly curtails upgrade cycles. Apple faces headwinds in US iPhone shipments as consumers defer purchases or shift to refurbished units, while Samsung's mid-range Galaxy lineup suffers similarly. Expect North American smartphone ASP erosion and cautious ODM order visibility through Q3 2026.

auto.zh: 製造業就業在12,580千人至12,605千人區間窄幅震盪，顯示工業勞動市場缺乏擴張動能。製造業工人為皮卡與入門SUV的主力買家，就業成長停滯導致汽車貸款申請量下降、次級信用違約率上升。Detroit三大車廠的高利潤輕型卡車銷售承壓，經銷商庫存天數攀升至70天以上。電動車方面，製造業就業疲軟抑制$40K-55K價位帶EV需求，Tesla Model 3與福特Mustang Mach-E訂單能見度轉弱，充電基礎設施投資回報期延長。
auto.en: Manufacturing employment oscillated narrowly between 12,580K and 12,605K over seven months, indicating stalled industrial labor market expansion. Blue-collar workers drive pickup and entry SUV demand; job stagnation reduces auto loan applications and elevates subprime delinquency rates. Detroit's Big Three face margin pressure as high-profit light truck sales soften, with dealer inventory days climbing above 70. For EVs, weak manufacturing employment suppresses $40K-55K segment demand—Tesla Model 3 and Ford Mustang Mach-E order visibility dims, while charging infrastructure ROI timelines extend as commercial fleet adoption by manufacturers slows.

tsmc.zh: 製造業就業從谷底回升7千人至12,605千人，但年減趨勢顯示美國工業客戶晶片需求分歧。對台積電而言，5nm/3nm先進製程受惠於AI加速器（NVIDIA H100/B200）與數據中心CPU持續投片，但28nm/16nm成熟製程面臨壓力—工業MCU、車用ADAS晶片、消費性IoT訂單因製造業資本支出保守而遞延。預期N3E產能利用率維持90%以上，但N7/N16投片量季減5-8%，客戶組合進一步向超大規模業者集中，加劇單一客戶風險。
tsmc.en: Manufacturing employment rose 7K to 12,605K from the December low, yet the year-over-year decline reveals bifurcated chip demand from US industrial clients. For TSMC, 5nm/3nm advanced nodes benefit from sustained AI accelerator (NVIDIA H100/B200) and datacenter CPU wafer starts, maintaining >90% utilization at N3E. However, 28nm/16nm mature nodes face headwinds—industrial MCU, automotive ADSAS, and consumer IoT orders deferred as manufacturing capex remains cautious. Expect N7/N16 wafer starts down 5-8% quarter-over-quarter, intensifying customer concentration risk toward hyperscalers and reducing diversification across traditional industrial and consumer electronics segments.

commentary.zh: 10年-2年公債利差持續在0.27%-0.40%的狹幅區間震盪，最新報0.31%。雖仍維持正值，但近日走勢疲弱，6月中旬的0.40%高點已回落。曲線仍未倒掛，惟利差持續壓低反映市場對經濟成長前景謹慎。若利差繼續收窄至零軸線下方，將成為衰退警訊。聯準會應密切監控曲線動態，作為調整政策的參考指標。
commentary.en: The 10Y-2Y spread remains positive at 0.31%, consolidating within a 0.27%-0.40% range since mid-June. Though not yet inverted—a reliable recession indicator—the recent downtrend from 0.40% signals market caution on growth. The compressed spread suggests investors are pricing modest economic headwinds ahead. The Fed should closely monitor further compression toward zero, as an inversion would historically precede recession by 6-18 months. Current levels warrant vigilance but not alarm.

ai.zh: 利差維持在+0.31%正值區間，顯示殖利率曲線未倒掛但斜率平緩。近期在0.27-0.4%窄幅震盪，反映經濟擴張後期特徵。AI資本支出（hyperscaler數據中心投資）短期仍受結構性需求支撐，但利差收窄至0.3%附近暗示未來6-12個月融資成本可能攀升，將壓縮NVIDIA/Microsoft等廠商的擴張性資本支出意願，GPU採購節奏可能在2027上半年放緩。
ai.en: The spread at +0.31% remains positive but compressed near recent lows (0.27-0.4% range), signaling late-cycle dynamics. While hyperscaler AI capex remains robust near-term due to structural GPU demand, the narrow spread suggests tightening financial conditions ahead. Rising long-term borrowing costs will pressure Microsoft, Google, and Meta's data center expansion budgets by H1 2027, potentially decelerating NVIDIA H100/B200 order momentum as CFOs prioritize ROI thresholds over capacity race.

mobile.zh: 利差自0.4%收窄至0.31%，雖仍為正值但趨勢向下，歷史上此型態領先消費性電子需求疲軟3-6個月。智慧型手機屬可選消費，當利差壓縮至0.3%區間時，消費者信心轉趨謹慎，換機週期延長。預期Apple iPhone與Samsung旗艦機在2026 Q4至2027 Q1出貨將面臨壓力，尤其新興市場分期付款利率上升將抑制中階機種需求，庫存調整風險增加。
mobile.en: Spread compression from 0.4% to 0.31% signals tightening credit conditions that historically precede consumer electronics softness by 3-6 months. Smartphone demand is discretionary; as spreads narrow toward 0.3%, upgrade cycles extend and installment financing becomes costlier in emerging markets. Apple and Samsung flagship shipments face headwinds in Q4 2026-Q1 2027, with mid-tier models particularly exposed to rising consumer financing rates. Inventory corrections likely by early 2027 as retail demand weakens.

auto.zh: 利差在+0.31%雖為正但持續低檔盤整（0.27-0.34%），反映信貸環境緊縮。汽車貸款利率與長天期公債連動，當10年期殖利率相對2年期優勢僅0.31個百分點時，車貸成本仍居高位，削弱購車負擔能力。預期2026 Q3-Q4美國輕型車銷售年增率放緩至個位數，電動車滲透率成長停滯，因消費者延後大額耐久財支出。Ford與GM庫存天數將攀升，供應鏈MCU與電池訂單下修。
auto.en: Spread at +0.31% remains compressed (0.27-0.34% range), keeping auto loan rates elevated as 10-year yields stay high relative to 2-year. Each 10bp spread narrowing adds ~$15/month to a $40K vehicle loan, dampening affordability. US light vehicle sales growth decelerates to low single digits in Q3-Q4 2026, with EV adoption stalling as consumers defer big-ticket purchases. Ford and GM inventory days climb; supply chain orders for MCUs and battery cells face downward revisions by year-end.

tsmc.zh: 利差+0.31%處於淺正值，對TSMC影響呈現結構分化。AI與HPC客戶（NVIDIA、AMD）的3nm/5nm先進製程需求短期維持滿載，但利差收窄預示2027年上半年終端需求降溫，智慧型手機與車用晶片（Apple A系列、NXP MCU）投片量將面臨下修壓力。TSMC資本支出outlook可能從2027年的320億美元下調至280-300億美元，成熟製程稼動率先行回落，客戶組合更依賴AI高毛利訂單。
tsmc.en: Spread at +0.31% creates bifurcated impact for TSMC. Near-term 3nm/5nm advanced node utilization remains tight driven by NVIDIA/AMD AI accelerators, but narrowing spread foreshadows H1 2027 demand softness in smartphone (Apple A-series) and automotive MCUs (NXP, Infineon). TSMC's 2027 capex guidance risks cut from $32B to $28-30B as mature node utilization dips first. Customer mix shifts further toward high-margin AI/HPC, while mobile and auto wafer starts face 10-15% downward revisions by Q1 2027.

commentary.zh: 10年-3月利差升至0.63%，較前期上升0.06個百分點。儘管最新讀數環比回升，但6月中旬以來整體呈下行趨勢，從0.68%滑落至0.51%低點後小幅反彈。該利差仍遠高於反轉門檻，暗示衰退風險目前溫和。然而，近期波動加劇反映市場對聯準會政策立場的不確定性。若利差持續走低或轉負，將為衰退預警信號，市場應密切關注後續聯準會決策與經濟數據動向。
commentary.en: The spread rebounded to 0.63%, up 0.06 percentage points, though it remains below mid-June levels of 0.68%. A notable dip to 0.51% on June 29th signals recent volatility. While the current level well above inversion thresholds suggests moderate recession risk, the recent downtrend warrants attention. As the Fed's preferred recession indicator with perfect historical accuracy, sustained weakness toward negative territory would signal heightened economic risk. Market participants should monitor forthcoming Fed communications and labor data closely.

ai.zh: 利差回升至0.63%（+0.06個百分點），從6月底0.51%低點反彈，顯示衰退風險降低。正斜率曲線支持AI基礎建設長期投資信心，有利NVIDIA、Microsoft、Google等超大規模業者維持2026-2027年資料中心擴張計畫。GPU訂單能見度改善，H100/B200需求穩固，雖利率仍屬中性區間，但曲線正常化趨勢減輕資本支出延遲壓力。
ai.en: Spread widened to 0.63% (+0.06pp), rebounding from 0.51% on June 29, signaling reduced recession risk. Positive slope supports long-duration AI infrastructure capex confidence for hyperscalers like NVIDIA, Microsoft, Google. Data center buildout plans for 2026-2027 remain intact, with H100/B200 GPU demand solid. Though rates remain in neutral territory, curve normalization eases fears of capex deferrals, improving order visibility for AI accelerators and high-bandwidth memory supply chains.

mobile.zh: 利差0.63%處於溫和正值，較月底0.51%回升，但波動於0.51-0.68%區間顯示經濟前景仍不明朗。智慧型手機屬可選消費，當前曲線斜率未明確刺激換機需求。Apple iPhone 17與Samsung Galaxy旗艦機銷售維持穩定但缺乏爆發力，AI手機功能尚未轉化為量的成長動能。消費者信心需利差持續擴大至1%以上才能帶動明顯復甦。
mobile.en: Spread at 0.63% (+0.06pp from 0.57%) reflects mild economic stability, but recent volatility (0.51-0.68% range) signals uncertain consumer outlook. Smartphones are discretionary; current curve slope insufficient to trigger upgrade cycles. Apple iPhone 17 and Samsung Galaxy flagship demand remains steady but lacks momentum. AI smartphone features not yet driving volume growth. Consumer confidence requires sustained spread expansion above 1% to meaningfully boost Apple/Samsung shipment growth beyond replacement demand.

auto.zh: 利差從0.57%升至0.63%，曲線正常化理論上降低衰退機率，但絕對水平仍偏低，汽車貸款利率維持5.5-7%高檔區間。傳統燃油車銷售受制於融資成本，皮卡與SUV需求疲軟。電動車方面，Tesla、BYD受惠政策補貼而非利率環境，但通用汽車、福特電動車部門因資金成本壓力延後部分產能擴充，供應鏈MCU與動力電池訂單成長放緩。
auto.en: Spread rising to 0.63% from 0.57% suggests lower recession risk, yet absolute level remains modest; auto loan rates stay elevated at 5.5-7%. Traditional ICE vehicle sales constrained by financing costs—pickup and SUV demand soft. EV adoption driven more by subsidies (Tesla, BYD) than rate environment. GM and Ford deferring some EV capacity expansion due to capital costs. Supply chain impact: MCU and battery orders growing slower than prior quarter, as 0.63% spread insufficient to ease financing headwinds materially.

tsmc.zh: 利差回升至0.63%減輕半導體週期下行疑慮，台積電3奈米與5奈米產能利用率維持高檔。AI晶片客戶（NVIDIA、AMD、博通）訂單穩健，抵銷手機與PC客戶疲軟。曲線正常化支持客戶2027年先進製程資本支出承諾，CoWoS先進封裝需求強勁。然利差未達1%以上，消費性晶片（手機應用處理器、IoT）復甦力道有限，客戶組合仍倚重HPC與AI。
tsmc.en: Spread widening to 0.63% (+0.06pp) eases semiconductor cycle downturn concerns; TSMC's 3nm and 5nm utilization rates remain elevated. AI chip customers (NVIDIA, AMD, Broadcom) provide stable orders, offsetting smartphone and PC softness. Curve normalization bolsters customer commitment to 2027 advanced-node capex; CoWoS advanced packaging demand robust. However, spread below 1% means consumer chip recovery (smartphone APs, IoT) stays muted. Customer mix remains heavily weighted to HPC and AI, with limited broadening to consumer segments.

commentary.zh: 10年期美債殖利率升至4.44%，較前期上漲6個基點。近一個月內波動區間在4.38%-4.51%，整體呈現震盪走勢。6月中旬一度跌至4.38%低點，隨後反彈至月底4.44%水準。殖利率回升反映市場對聯準會降息進程的疑慮，以及通膨預期的重新評估。上升的殖利率將對房貸成本和企業借貸成本產生上行壓力，同時壓縮高本益比科技股的估值空間，投資人應密切監測殖利率的後續走向。
commentary.en: The 10-year Treasury yield rose to 4.44%, up 6 basis points, remaining within a narrow band of 4.38%-4.51% over the past month. After touching a low of 4.38% mid-June, it rebounded to month-end levels. The uptick reflects renewed Fed rate-cut hesitation and inflation reassessment. Rising yields will pressure mortgage and corporate borrowing costs while compressing valuations for duration-sensitive growth stocks. Market participants should monitor whether yields stabilize or face further upside pressure amid evolving monetary policy expectations.

ai.zh: 10年期殖利率從4.38%反彈至4.44%（+6bps），對資本密集的AI產業構成壓力。超大規模雲端業者如Microsoft、Google的數據中心資本支出折現價值下降，債務融資成本上升將壓縮AI基建擴張速度。NVIDIA等高估值AI股票面臨本益比壓縮風險，儘管GPU需求依然強勁，但殖利率上行趨勢（6月中以來波動於4.38-4.51%區間）持續抑制長天期現金流標的估值。
ai.en: The 10-year yield rebounding to 4.44% (+6bps from 4.38%) pressures capital-intensive AI infrastructure. Hyperscalers like Microsoft and Google face higher debt financing costs for data center capex, reducing NPV of multi-year AI buildouts. NVIDIA and high-multiple AI stocks see valuation compression despite robust GPU demand. The recent range of 4.38-4.51% since late June signals persistent discount rate headwinds for long-duration tech cash flows, potentially delaying some hyperscaler capex decisions.

mobile.zh: 殖利率升至4.44%對手機產業呈負面影響。消費者融資成本上升削弱高階iPhone和Galaxy旗艦機的分期購買意願，尤其在美國市場約40%智慧型手機透過電信商融資方案銷售。從6月底4.38%低點回升6個基點，顯示借貸成本維持高檔，不利於換機週期復甦。Apple和Samsung的平均售價策略面臨壓力，可能導致消費者轉向中階機型，壓縮產品組合獲利率。
mobile.en: The yield rising to 4.44% dampens smartphone demand through higher consumer financing costs. In the US, approximately 40% of smartphones are sold via carrier installment plans—the 6bp increase from 4.38% makes premium iPhone and Galaxy flagships less affordable on monthly payment terms. The sustained 4.4%+ level since late June delays upgrade cycles and pressures Apple/Samsung ASPs as consumers downgrade to mid-tier models, compressing product mix margins and shipment growth expectations.

auto.zh: 殖利率從4.38%上升至4.44%直接推高汽車貸款利率，削弱購車需求。新車平均貸款利率隨之攀升至7-8%區間，月付款壓力抑制燃油車與電動車銷售。EV採用特別受創，因電動車單價較高且買家對利率敏感度更強。傳統車廠與Tesla的庫存天數可能增加，迫使提供更多促銷優惠。較高融資成本也延遲車隊電動化投資，供應鏈零組件需求（MCU、電池）面臨下修風險。
auto.en: The yield climbing from 4.38% to 4.44% directly raises auto loan rates, now approaching 7-8% for new vehicles, increasing monthly payments and dampening sales. EV adoption is particularly vulnerable due to higher price points and financing sensitivity—Tesla and legacy OEMs face rising inventory days, forcing incentive programs. The 6bp increase since June 26 signals sustained financing headwinds, delaying fleet electrification capex and softening supply chain demand for MCUs, battery cells, and semiconductor content per vehicle.

tsmc.zh: 殖利率回升至4.44%對台積電呈現雙面影響。負面方面，主要客戶（Apple手機、AMD/NVIDIA AI晶片）估值承壓，可能延後先進製程投片量，3nm/2nm產能利用率面臨不確定性。正向方面，AI伺服器晶片需求（NVIDIA H100/B100、Google TPU）相對剛性，支撐CoWoS先進封裝滿載。台積電2026年資本支出（約300億美元）的融資成本雖上升，但殖利率4.4%仍處可控範圍，技術領先地位持續吸引客戶長約承諾。
tsmc.en: The yield at 4.44% creates mixed dynamics for TSMC. Negatively, key customers—Apple (smartphones), AMD/NVIDIA (AI chips)—face valuation pressure and potential wafer order delays, risking 3nm/2nm utilization rates. Positively, AI server chip demand (NVIDIA H100/B100, Google TPU) remains inelastic, keeping CoWoS advanced packaging at full capacity. TSMC's ~$30B 2026 capex faces modestly higher financing costs, but 4.44% yields remain manageable, and technology leadership sustains long-term customer commitments despite rate volatility in the 4.38-4.51% recent range.

commentary.zh: VIX於6月30日報16.45，環比下跌1.2點至-6.8%，落在15-20的自滿區間下緣。近月波動呈現溫和起伏，自6月中旬16.2點攀升至6月23日高點19.49點，隨後回落。當前水位表明市場風險情緒趨穩，投資人對股市前景信心恢復。惟需關注美聯儲政策走向與經濟數據發布，若通膨再起或利率決策生變，VIX恐重新突破警戒線，觸發避險買盤。
commentary.en: VIX closed at 16.45 on June 30, down 1.2 points (-6.8%), settling in the complacency zone below 15-20. After spiking to 19.49 on June 23, volatility has moderated steadily. Current levels suggest investor risk appetite is stabilizing and equity market confidence is recovering. However, watch Fed policy signals and economic data releases closely—inflation surprises or hawkish rate guidance could push VIX above 20, triggering flight-to-safety demand.

ai.zh: VIX從19.49高點回落至16.45（降6.8%），顯示市場波動緩和，有利AI資本密集投資。風險偏好回升支持NVIDIA、Microsoft、Google等hyperscaler持續GPU採購與資料中心擴建。目前16.45處於低波動區間，機構投資人對長週期AI基礎建設支出信心增強，CoWoS先進封裝與H100/B200訂單能見度提升。
ai.en: VIX declining from 19.49 to 16.45 (-6.8%) signals reduced market stress, favorable for capital-intensive AI infrastructure spending. Lower volatility supports hyperscalers' GPU procurement commitments and data center capex. At 16.45, institutional confidence in multi-year AI buildouts strengthens, benefiting NVIDIA's order backlog, Microsoft/Google cloud expansion, and CoWoS advanced packaging demand for H100/B200 chips.

mobile.zh: VIX降至16.45反映市場情緒改善，但手機屬成熟消費品，對波動率敏感度低於科技資本支出。近期VIX從18-19區間回穩，可能輕微提振高階旗艦機（iPhone 16 Pro、Galaxy S26）買氣，但換機週期延長至3.5年與中國市場疲弱仍是主要壓力。整體出貨量受惠有限，sentiment僅溫和中性。
mobile.en: VIX drop to 16.45 reflects calmer markets, but smartphone demand shows low correlation to volatility given mature product cycles. Decline from 18-19 range may marginally boost premium flagship sentiment (iPhone 16 Pro, Galaxy S26), yet extended replacement cycles (3.5 years) and China weakness persist. Shipment volumes see limited uplift; overall impact remains neutral amid structural headwinds.

auto.zh: VIX回落至16.45降低融資成本擔憂，有利汽車貸款利差收窄，支持傳統燃油車與EV銷售。然而從17.65進一步下探僅1.2點，改善幅度溫和。北美pickup與SUV融資需求可能回溫，但EV庫存高企（60天以上）與Tesla降價競爭仍施壓利潤率。供應鏈方面車用MCU需求回穩，整體sentiment偏mixed。
auto.en: VIX falling to 16.45 eases financing concerns, tightening auto loan spreads and supporting both ICE and EV sales. However, the 1.2-point drop from 17.65 is modest. North American pickup/SUV financing may improve, but elevated EV inventory (60+ days) and Tesla price cuts pressure margins. Automotive MCU demand stabilizes with lower volatility, yielding mixed sentiment amid uneven recovery across segments.

tsmc.zh: VIX從6月高點19.49降至16.45，波動率壓縮改善晶圓代工能見度。3nm/5nm先進製程利用率受惠於AI加速器（NVIDIA H200、AMD MI300）與iPhone A18訂單穩定，CoWoS月產能擴至7萬片支撐毛利率。低VIX環境下客戶如Apple、AMD、Broadcom的2027年先進封裝commitment更明確，資本支出outlook維持280-320億美元高檔。
tsmc.en: VIX declining from 19.49 peak to 16.45 enhances wafer foundry visibility. 3nm/5nm advanced node utilization benefits from stable AI accelerator orders (NVIDIA H200, AMD MI300) and iPhone A18 demand, with CoWoS capacity expanding to 70k/month supporting gross margins. Lower volatility clarifies 2027 advanced packaging commitments from Apple, AMD, Broadcom, maintaining TSMC's capex outlook at $28-32B.

commentary.zh: 高收益債利差升至275bps，雖較前日下跌5個基點，但仍反映近期上升趨勢。6月中旬低點266bps後，利差已累積上升約9bps，顯示市場風險偏好逐漸退溫。儘管仍遠低於400bps壓力水位，但上升軌跡值得關注，暗示投資人對信用風險的警覺性提升。若利差持續走高突破300bps，可能預示經濟前景疑慮或流動性緊張，將成為聯準會評估政策調整的重要參考指標。
commentary.en: High yield spreads widened to 275bps, down 5bps from prior day but reflecting an upward trend since mid-June's 266bps trough. The 9bps cumulative rise signals moderating risk appetite, though well below the 400bps stress threshold. This gradual widening warrants monitoring as an early warning of credit concerns or liquidity tightening. A sustained break above 300bps could indicate economic headwinds, influencing Fed policy considerations.

ai.zh: 高收益債利差275bps遠低於400bps風險偏好門檻，且較前期下降5bps至-1.8%，顯示信用市場寬鬆，有利超大規模業者(Microsoft/Google)以低成本發債融資AI資料中心建設。緊縮利差降低NVIDIA客戶的資本成本，支撐GPU採購預算及長期AI基礎設施投資。信用循環擴張期間，高風險AI新創亦較易取得成長資金，加速算力需求成長。
ai.en: OAS at 275bps, well below the 400bps risk-on threshold and tightening 5bps (-1.8%), signals robust credit conditions favoring hyperscaler debt issuance for data center capex. Lower spreads reduce financing costs for Microsoft/Google's multi-billion AI infrastructure builds, sustaining GPU procurement from NVIDIA. Benign credit cycle also enables AI startups to access growth capital more easily, compounding compute demand across the ecosystem and supporting premium pricing for accelerators.

mobile.zh: 利差275bps反映穩健信用環境，但手機為成熟消費品，需求更受實質收入而非融資成本驅動。近期利差從283bps波動回落至275bps，未見明確趨勢方向，對Apple/Samsung出貨量影響有限。消費者少透過高收益債市融資購機，該指標對換機週期的指示性弱於就業與薪資數據，維持中性展望。
mobile.en: Spread at 275bps reflects stable credit, but smartphones are discretionary purchases driven by real income, not high-yield financing. Recent volatility (283bps on 6/26 to 275bps now) shows no clear trend impacting Apple/Samsung shipments. Consumers don't finance phones via junk bonds; upgrade cycles depend more on employment and wages. The tightening spread offers minimal read-through to handset demand, warranting a neutral stance on mobile volumes.

auto.zh: 利差收窄至275bps並下降5bps，改善汽車金融公司(captive finance arms)及次級購車者的融資條件。較低信用利差壓縮汽車貸款利率，降低每月付款負擔，刺激一般消費者購車及EV升級意願。供應鏈端，零組件供應商舉債成本下降，有利庫存融資及產能擴張。唯需監控利差是否持穩於低檔以確認趨勢延續。
auto.en: Spread tightening to 275bps (-5bps) lowers funding costs for captive finance arms and subprime auto lenders, translating into cheaper car loans and lease rates. Monthly payment relief supports vehicle sales, including EV adoption where financing is critical due to higher sticker prices. Tier-2 suppliers also benefit from reduced borrowing costs for inventory and capex. However, recent volatility (peaked 283bps on 6/26) requires monitoring to confirm sustained trend supportive of auto demand.
