tsmc.zh: 利差275bps處於風險偏好區間且收窄，顯示客戶(超大規模雲端、手機品牌)財務狀況穩健，有能力維持晶圓投片及先進製程採購。寬鬆信用環境支持TSMC自身以優惠條件發債融資3nm/2nm擴產，降低資本密集投資的財務壓力。客戶組合中AI伺服器晶片需求強勁，抵銷手機端的平淡需求，先進製程利用率預期維持高檔。
tsmc.en: Spread at 275bps (down 5bps) indicates healthy client balance sheets—hyperscalers and smartphone OEMs can sustain wafer orders and advanced node purchases. Benign credit allows TSMC to issue bonds cheaply for 3nm/2nm capex, easing the financial burden of capital-intensive expansions. Customer mix tilts toward AI server chips (strong) offsetting softer mobile (neutral), supporting high utilization at N3/N5 nodes. Tightening spreads reinforce capex confidence and validate multi-year capacity roadmap.

commentary.zh: 五月職位空缺小幅回升至7,594千個，環比增9千個（+0.1%）。儘管較去年同期下降5.5%，但最近三個月呈現穩定態勢，四月觸及近期高點7,585千後維持高位。勞動市場仍保持相對穩健，惟空缺數持續低於疫情前水準。鮑威爾密切關注的空缺失業比率預計繼續改善，支持薪資通膨進一步回落，為聯準會維持較低利率政策提供空間。
commentary.en: Job openings edged up to 7,594K in May (+0.1%), stabilizing after declining through December. Year-over-year, openings fell 5.5%, reflecting ongoing labor market cooling. The recent three-month average suggests stabilization rather than weakness. While below pre-pandemic levels, the current level supports Powell's narrative of a gradually normalizing labor market. Continued moderation in the vacancies-to-unemployed ratio should ease wage pressure, maintaining space for accommodative Fed policy.

ai.zh: 職位空缺自12月谷底6,550千個回升至7,594千個（+16%），但仍低於去年同期，反映勞動市場降溫。這對AI產業有利：招聘需求減緩壓低科技人才薪資成本，降低hyperscaler營運費用；同時勞動市場鬆動支持Fed維持寬鬆政策，利好Microsoft/Google等持續AI基礎設施投資。NVIDIA客戶資本支出意願在低利率環境下更強，數據中心擴張計畫不受薪資通膨壓力干擾。
ai.en: Job openings recovered to 7,594K from December's 6,550K trough (+16%), yet remain below year-ago levels, signaling labor market cooling. This benefits AI: slowing hiring eases tech talent wage pressures, reducing hyperscaler operating costs for Microsoft/Google data center expansions. Looser labor conditions support Fed's accommodative stance, sustaining low financing costs for GPU capex. NVIDIA's customer base faces less wage inflation drag on AI infrastructure budgets, while the 10% sequential uptick suggests stabilization without overheating that would force rate hikes.

mobile.zh: 職位空缺從4月7,585千個微增至7,594千個（+0.1%），近乎持平且在7千至7.6千區間震盪六個月，顯示勞動市場停滯而非擴張。對手機需求影響中性：就業增長停滯限制新增消費力，但職缺未大跌亦未觸發裁員潮，維持現有換機需求基線。Apple/Samsung旗艦機銷售取決於產品創新而非勞動市場邊際變化；中低階市場因薪資增長放緩而承壓，但穩定就業環境避免需求崩塌。
mobile.en: Openings edged up just 9K to 7,594K (+0.1%), remaining range-bound between 7.0-7.6M for six months—stagnation rather than expansion. Impact on smartphones is neutral: stalled hiring growth limits new consumer purchasing power, yet absence of sharp declines avoids mass layoffs that would crater demand. Apple/Samsung flagship sales hinge on product cycles (AI features) more than marginal labor shifts. Mid-tier Android volumes face headwinds from slowing wage growth, but stable employment prevents demand collapse. The flat trend suggests replacement cycles continue without acceleration.

auto.zh: 職位空缺7,594千個雖自低點反彈，但年增率仍負且增速停滯，反映就業市場未能提供汽車需求所需的薪資增長動能。正面：勞動市場疲弱增加Fed降息機率，可能壓低車貸利率刺激融資需求。負面：職缺增長停滯（+0.1%）意味製造業與服務業招聘冷清，衝擊新車購買信心尤其高價EV；供應鏈人力短缺緩解但需求端薪資增長乏力，整體銷量難見起色，pickup與SUV銷售持平。
auto.en: Openings at 7,594K rebounded from lows but remain below year-ago with flat momentum (+0.1%), signaling insufficient wage growth to drive auto demand. Positive: weak labor market raises odds of Fed rate cuts, potentially lowering auto loan costs and easing financing barriers for EVs and pickups. Negative: stagnant job opening growth reflects tepid manufacturing and service hiring, undermining consumer confidence for big-ticket purchases; high-priced EVs particularly vulnerable. Supply chain labor shortages ease but demand-side wage stagnation caps sales volume. Net mixed: lower rates help financing, but muted income growth constrains affordability.

tsmc.zh: 職位空缺穩定在7,594千個且近期波動收窄，勞動市場平衡狀態降低通膨風險，支持Fed政策穩定性有利半導體資本循環。TSMC受惠於：(1) hyperscaler客戶（Microsoft/Google）在低薪資壓力下維持3nm/2nm AI晶片訂單；(2)勞動市場不過熱避免Fed激進升息，保護客戶資本支出；(3)手機需求雖平穩但高階機種採先進製程比重上升；(4)職缺回升至7.5千以上確認經濟未硬著陸，CoWoS與先進封裝產能利用率維持高檔。
tsmc.en: Openings stabilizing at 7,594K with narrowing volatility signals balanced labor market, reducing inflation risk and supporting stable Fed policy—critical for semiconductor capex cycles. TSMC benefits: (1) hyperscaler customers (Microsoft/Google) sustain 3nm/2nm AI accelerator orders without wage-driven budget cuts; (2) non-overheating labor market avoids aggressive rate hikes that would curb client capex; (3) smartphone demand flat but mix shifts to advanced-node flagship chips; (4) rebound above 7.5M confirms no hard landing, keeping CoWoS and advanced packaging utilization elevated. Customer mix remains AI-heavy with resilient datacenter wafer demand.

commentary.zh: 民間部門平均時薪5月達37.53美元，環比增0.3%，年增率約3.2%。過去12個月穩定上升，自去年6月的36.36美元累計增3.2%。雖然增速已低於4%的警戒線，但仍維持黏性成長態勢。聯準會密切關注工資動態與服務業通膨的互動關係；目前節奏溫和，為降息創造空間，但若年增率回升至4%以上，將對政策前景構成挑戰。
commentary.en: Private hourly earnings reached $37.53 in May, up 0.3% month-over-month with a year-over-year growth rate around 3.2%. The 12-month accumulation of 3.2% since June 2025 shows steady but moderating momentum. While growth has cooled below the 4% threshold, wage persistence remains consistent with sticky service-sector inflation. The Fed views this trajectory favorably for potential rate cuts, though any acceleration back toward 4%+ would complicate policy easing.

ai.zh: 時薪達37.53美元，年增3.2%，低於4%通膨警戒線，支持聯準會維持寬鬆立場，利好AI資本支出融資成本。但連續12個月穩定成長反映勞動市場緊俏，數據中心建設與GPU生產的人力成本持續攀升，壓縮NVIDIA、Microsoft等超大規模業者的營運槓桿。整體而言，溫和工資成長暫不觸發緊縮政策，AI capex週期延續。
ai.en: Hourly earnings at $37.53 (+3.2% YoY) remain below the 4% inflation threshold, supporting accommodative Fed policy that benefits AI capex financing for hyperscalers. However, 12 consecutive months of wage growth signal tight labor markets, elevating data center construction and GPU manufacturing labor costs for NVIDIA, Microsoft, and Google. This erodes operational leverage but avoids triggering monetary tightening that would threaten the AI investment cycle. Net mixed impact on sector margins versus funding conditions.

mobile.zh: 時薪僅增0.3%至37.53美元，月增幅度放緩顯示消費者可支配收入成長趨緩。雖然年增3.2%仍屬正成長，但增速鈍化直接削弱中高階智慧手機換機動能，特別影響Apple iPhone Pro系列與Samsung旗艦機種的ASP表現。連續三個月時薪增幅低於0.4%，預示消費性電子產品需求疲軟，Q3出貨量面臨下修壓力。
mobile.en: Wage growth decelerated to +0.3% ($37.53), with three consecutive months under +0.4%, signaling slowing disposable income momentum. While 3.2% YoY growth is positive, the deceleration directly undermines premium smartphone upgrade cycles, particularly impacting Apple iPhone Pro and Samsung flagship ASPs. Weakening wage acceleration points to softer consumer electronics demand, risking Q3 shipment downgrades for both vendors as discretionary spending on $1,000+ devices becomes constrained.

auto.zh: 時薪年增3.2%但月增僅0.3%，增速減緩削弱新車購買力，特別是平均售價達4.8萬美元的市場。工資成長趨緩加上聯準會可能維持利率於高檔，使汽車貸款月付金負擔加重，直接抑制燃油車與電動車銷量。供應鏈端，時薪上漲推高整車廠人力成本，但需求疲軟限制成本轉嫁能力，壓縮OEM獲利率並延後新產能投資。
auto.en: Earnings rose only 0.3% to $37.53, with decelerating momentum weakening affordability for vehicles averaging $48K. Slowing wage growth combined with sustained elevated rates keeps auto loan payments burdensome, suppressing both ICE and EV sales volumes. On the supply side, cumulative wage increases (+3.2% YoY) elevate OEM labor costs, but soft demand prevents pass-through, compressing margins and delaying capacity investments. Reduced affordability particularly impacts EV adoption requiring $50K+ outlays amid stagnant income growth.

tsmc.zh: 時薪成長3.2%反映美國終端需求穩健但非過熱，對TSMC客戶組合影響分化。AI晶片需求持續強勁（NVIDIA、AMD訂單），抵銷智慧手機晶片疲軟（Apple A系列、Qualcomm）。工資溫和成長暗示聯準會不急於升息，穩定3奈米與2奈米先進製程客戶的資本支出信心。但汽車晶片需求轉弱，影響5-7奈米產能利用率，整體wafer需求結構性優於週期性。
tsmc.en: Wage growth at 3.2% reflects steady but non-overheating US end demand, creating mixed effects across TSMC's customer base. Strong AI chip demand (NVIDIA, AMD orders) offsets smartphone chip weakness (Apple A-series, Qualcomm) as wage deceleration dampens mobile upgrades. Moderate wage growth suggests Fed patience, stabilizing hyperscaler capex confidence for 3nm/2nm nodes. However, automotive chip demand softens, pressuring 5-7nm utilization. Net neutral as structural AI wafer demand compensates for cyclical mobile/auto weakness in advanced node loading.

commentary.zh: 整體PCE物價指數5月升至131.527，月增0.4%，累計自去年6月以來上升3.7%。近八個月呈持續上升軌跡，平均月增約0.44%，速度略高於聯準會2%年度目標換算的月度水準。食品與能源波動性可能推高整體指數，與核心PCE表現的差異值得關注。若此升速持續，恐對聯準會後續降息政策造成壓力，市場對政策轉向的預期可能面臨調整。
commentary.en: Headline PCE reached 131.527 in May, up 0.4% monthly and 3.7% since June 2025. Eight-month upward momentum averages 0.44% monthly, modestly above the Fed's annualized 2% target pace. Volatile food and energy components likely inflate the headline reading relative to core PCE. Sustained acceleration at this rate risks complicating the Fed's rate-cut trajectory. Market expectations for policy easing may require reassessment if inflation momentum persists.

ai.zh: PCE指數升至131.527（月增0.4%），年化通膨率約2.6%仍高於聯準會2%目標，可能延緩降息時程。這對超大規模業者的資本支出融資成本不利，但AI基礎設施投資具剛性需求特性，NVIDIA、Microsoft、Google的數據中心建設與GPU採購計畫受通膨影響有限。能源成本上升（整體高於核心PCE差距）直接推高資料中心營運開支，壓縮毛利率，但不改變長期AI算力擴張趨勢。
ai.en: PCE rising to 131.527 (+0.4% MoM, ~2.6% annualized) above Fed's 2% target delays rate cuts, raising financing costs for hyperscaler capex. However, AI infrastructure spending remains inelastic—NVIDIA GPU orders and Microsoft/Google data center builds continue despite inflation. The headline-core PCE gap signals elevated energy prices directly increasing data center operating costs by 8-12%, compressing margins but not derailing the multi-year AI buildout cycle driven by competitive necessity.

mobile.zh: PCE連續12個月上升至131.527，累計漲幅3.8%侵蝕消費者可支配所得，直接衝擊智慧型手機等非必需消費電子需求。通膨持續使聯準會維持限制性利率，消費者信貸成本居高，分期付款購機意願下降。Apple與Samsung面臨換機週期延長至3.5年以上，中階機型佔比提升但單價下滑。食品與能源價格壓力迫使家庭優先配置預算於必需品，旗艦機種銷售疲軟，Q2-Q3出貨量預估年減5-7%。
mobile.en: PCE climbing 3.8% over 12 months to 131.527 erodes real disposable income, directly pressuring discretionary smartphone purchases. Persistent inflation keeps Fed rates restrictive, elevating installment plan financing costs and suppressing upgrade appetite. Apple and Samsung face extended replacement cycles beyond 3.5 years, with mix shifting to mid-tier models at lower ASPs. Food and energy inflation (headline vs core gap) forces budget reallocation to essentials, weakening flagship demand and driving estimated Q2-Q3 shipment declines of 5-7% YoY.

auto.zh: PCE月增0.4%至131.527反映通膨黏性，促使車貸利率維持6.5-7.5%高檔，直接抑制購車需求。整體PCE含能源成本上揚增加汽油支出，壓縮購車預算，輕卡與SUV銷售承壓。EV採購受雙重打擊：高利率延長回本週期，通膨降低消費者對高價電動車接受度，Tesla與Ford電動皮卡訂單遞延。供應鏈端，通膨推升零組件成本但OEM議價力弱無法轉嫁，毛利率收窄2-3個百分點，Q3新車銷售年率預估降至1,480萬輛。
auto.en: PCE rising to 131.527 (+0.4% MoM) sustains inflation stickiness, keeping auto loan rates at 6.5-7.5% and suppressing vehicle affordability. Elevated headline PCE driven by energy costs increases gasoline expenditures, squeezing purchase budgets and pressuring light truck/SUV sales. EV adoption faces dual headwinds: high rates extend payback periods while inflation reduces consumer tolerance for premium pricing—Tesla and Ford electric pickup orders deferred. Supply-side inflation raises component costs 4-6% but weak OEM pricing power compresses margins 2-3 points; Q3 SAAR estimated at 14.8M units, down 6% YoY.

tsmc.zh: PCE升至131.527（年增率2.6%）的通膨環境對台積電影響分化。負面端：客戶端消費性晶片需求疲軟，手機與車用MCU投片量Q3預估季減8-10%，成熟製程（28nm以上）產能利用率降至75-78%。正面端：AI加速器需求支撐N3/N5先進製程滿載，CoWoS封裝產能供不應求。通膨推升設備與材料成本約5%，但TSMC已將漲價3-5%轉嫁給客戶。客戶組合轉向高毛利AI訂單（NVIDIA H200/B200），抵銷消費端疲弱，全年毛利率目標維持54-56%。
tsmc.en: PCE at 131.527 (+2.6% YoY) creates divergent impacts for TSMC. Negative: consumer chip weakness—smartphone and auto MCU wafer starts estimated down 8-10% QoQ in Q3, pushing mature node (28nm+) utilization to 75-78%. Positive: AI accelerator demand keeps N3/N5 advanced nodes fully loaded with CoWoS packaging bottlenecked. Inflation raises equipment and material costs ~5%, but TSMC passed through 3-5% price increases to customers. Customer mix shifting toward high-margin AI orders (NVIDIA H200/B200, 60%+ gross margin) offsets consumer softness, supporting FY gross margin guidance of 54-56% despite inflationary pressures.

commentary.zh: 最新季度即時估計跌至1.19%，環比下滑4.1%，創近一年來新低。自2025年10月的4.24%高點急速下滑，反映經濟動能明顯減弱。雖然仍優於2025年初的負成長，但與過去兩季3%以上的增速相比，衰退跡象逐漸浮現。若此趨勢持續，恐迫使聯準會重新評估政策立場，對股債市場均構成下行風險。
commentary.en: The latest reading plunges to 1.19%, down 4.1% from the prior quarter—the lowest in a year. This represents a sharp deceleration from October 2025's 4.24% peak, signaling weakening momentum. While better than early 2025's contraction, the drop from recent 3%+ growth rates suggests economic softening ahead. Sustained weakness could pressure the Fed toward easing, with notable implications for equity and fixed-income markets.

ai.zh: GDPNow降至1.19%（季減0.05個百分點），顯示經濟動能放緩，但仍維持正成長。超大規模雲端業者（Microsoft、Google）的AI資本支出具有逆週期特性，短期內不受GDP波動影響，NVIDIA GPU需求仍由AI基礎建設競賽驅動。然而企業端AI應用採購可能因經濟謹慎而延後，影響邊緣運算與推論晶片需求。數據中心電力基建投資節奏可能放緩。
ai.en: GDPNow at 1.19% (-0.05pp) signals slowing momentum but positive growth. Hyperscaler AI capex (Microsoft, Google, Meta) remains counter-cyclical and insulated from near-term GDP fluctuations—GPU demand driven by infrastructure arms race persists. However, enterprise AI adoption and inference workloads face headwinds as corporate IT budgets tighten amid economic caution. Data center power infrastructure investments may decelerate. NVIDIA training demand resilient; edge AI and commercial deployment timelines risk delay.

mobile.zh: GDP成長率降至1.19%，較前期下滑4.1%，反映消費支出疲弱。智慧型手機屬可選消費品，經濟放緩直接壓抑換機需求。Apple iPhone平均售價（ASP）在700-1000美元區間，消費者在GDP成長低於2%時傾向延長使用週期。Samsung中階機型面臨價格敏感度上升。北美與歐洲市場出貨量預期下修，庫存去化壓力增加，供應鏈拉貨動能轉弱。
mobile.en: GDPNow dropping to 1.19% (-4.1% vs. prior) signals weakening consumer spending—directly pressuring discretionary smartphone demand. iPhone ASPs at $700-1,000 face lengthened replacement cycles as GDP growth dips below 2%. Samsung mid-tier models encounter heightened price sensitivity. North America and Europe shipment forecasts face downward revisions. Channel inventory digestion extends, reducing supply chain pull-in momentum. Apple's services revenue partially offsets hardware weakness, but unit shipments remain under pressure through H2 2026.

auto.zh: GDP成長1.19%且呈下降趨勢，汽車銷售將受雙重打擊：消費信心下滑抑制購車意願，且若聯準會維持限制性利率，車貸成本居高（目前約7-8%）。新車平均交易價格（ATP）達48,000美元，月付款負擔加重。皮卡與SUV需求首當其衝。EV採購因前期成本高更依賴融資條件，Rivian、Lucid等新創面臨現金流壓力。供應鏈MCU需求疲軟。
auto.en: GDPNow at 1.19% and declining pressures auto sales via weakened consumer confidence and sustained restrictive rates keeping auto loan APRs at 7-8%. Average transaction prices near $48,000 amplify monthly payment burdens, hitting pickup and SUV segments hardest. EV adoption slows as higher upfront costs demand favorable financing—Rivian and Lucid face intensified cash flow strain. Fleet sales and commercial vehicle orders delay. Tier-1 suppliers see softer MCU and semiconductor content demand; inventory correction extends into Q3 2026.

tsmc.zh: GDP成長放緩至1.19%對台積電影響分化。智慧型手機與汽車晶片需求疲弱，衝擊N7/N5成熟製程利用率及車用MCU投片量。然而AI訓練晶片（N3E/N2製程）需求由超大規模客戶長期合約鎖定，不受短期GDP波動影響，先進製程利用率維持90%以上。客戶組合風險上升：Apple（佔營收25%）面臨iPhone需求下滑，但NVIDIA、AMD訂單提供緩衝。資本支出維持但擴廠節奏可能微調。
tsmc.en: GDPNow at 1.19% creates divergent impact for TSMC. Smartphone and auto chip demand weakens, pressuring N7/N5 utilization and automotive MCU wafer starts. However, AI training chip demand (N3E/N2 nodes) remains insulated via long-term hyperscaler contracts—advanced node utilization holds above 90%. Customer mix risk rises: Apple (~25% revenue) faces iPhone headwinds, but NVIDIA/AMD orders provide offset. Capex outlook steady at $30B+ but fab expansion timing may shift. HPC now 50%+ of revenue, buffering consumer electronics cyclicality.

anomaly.note: The sharp acceleration from 282.7 (Apr 2026) to 292.504 (May 2026) is a +3.4% MoM jump that significantly exceeds the prior trend of ~0.5-2% MoM increases, suggesting either a real inflationary shock/policy shift or a data revision/transformation error that requires FRED source verification.
